Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) decree in the early Meiji era, the shrine's name changed from "Rokusha Daimyōjin" to "Rokusha Shrine" in 1874 (Meiji 7). It was later renamed "Roku Shrine," and the current main hall, rebuilt in 1890 (Meiji 23), was renovated with a copper roof between 1906 and 1907. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美大神}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞男大神}} * {{ill|Tsukuyomi|ja|月夜見大神}} * {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子大神}} == Sub-Shrines == * Ōtoshisha (豊宇気比賣命) * Shirahige-sha (Kifune Taisha) * Ebisu-miya (Hiruko Taisha) * Inari Omatsu-sha (Kurainatama Taisha) * Inari Gosha (Inari Ōkami) * Sugawara-sha (Sugawara Taisha) * Daikoku-miya ({{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}) == Festivals == The annual autumn festival is held on the second Sunday of October, with a preparatory night festival on the preceding Saturday. Festivities include mikoshi (portable shrines) and {{ill|Futondaiko|ja|布団太鼓}} processions. A traditional boat procession was previously part of the celebrations but is no longer conducted. Other festivals, such as Setsubun and summer festivals, are also held. === Futon Daiko === The original futon daiko was lost in a storage fire, leaving only mikoshi for the autumn festival for many years. The current futon daiko was acquired from {{ill|Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|ja|岩屋神社 (明石市)}}, which donated it after commissioning a new one. The "Futon Daiko Maiko Association" oversees its management and parade. == Access == * Nearest station: {{ill|Nishi-Maiko Station|ja|西舞子駅}} on the {{ill|Sanyo Electric Railway|ja|山陽電気鉄道}}, 3 minutes on foot; The shrine's deities are the same as those of the {{ill|Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|ja|岩屋神社 (明石市)}} in Akashi, suggesting that its foundation dates to the early Edo period, when spirits from Iwaya Shrine were enshrined here. The shrine served as the general {{ill|Chinju|ja|鎮守}} (village guardian) of Yamada Village in Harima Province. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buqqhism) decree in the early Meiji era, the shrine's name changed from "Rokusha Daimyōjin" to "Rokusha Shrine" in 1874 (Meiji 7). It was later renamed "Roku Shrine," and the current main hall, rebuilt in 1890 (Meiji 23), was renovated with a copper roof between 1906 and 1907. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美大神}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞男大神}} * {{ill|Tsukuyomi|ja|月夜見大神}} * {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子大神}} == Sub-Shrines == * Ōtoshisha (豊宇気比賣命) * Shirahige-sha (Kifune Taisha) * Ebisu-miya (Hiruko Taisha) * Inari Omatsu-sha (Kurainatama Taisha) * Inari Gosha (Inari Ōkami) * Sugawara-sha (Sugawara Taisha) * Daikoku-miya ({{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}) == Festivals == The annual autumn festival is held on the second Sunday of October, with a preparatory night festival on the preceding Saturday. Festivities include mikoshi (portable shrines) and {{ill|Futondaiko|ja|布団太鼓}} processions. A traditional boat procession was previously part of the celebrations but is no longer conducted. Other festivals, such as Setsubun and summer festivals, are also held. === Futon Daiko === The original futon daiko was lost in a storage fire, leaving only mikoshi for the autumn festival for many years. The current futon daiko was acquired from {{ill|Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|ja|岩屋神社 (明石市)}}, which donated it after commissioning a new one. The "Futon Daiko Maiko Association" oversees its management and parade. == Access == * Nearest station: {{ill|Nishi-Maiko Station|ja|西舞子駅}} on the {{ill|Sanyo Electric Railway|ja|山陽電気鉄道}}, 3 minutes on foot; or {{ill|Maiko Station|ja|舞子駅}} on the JR line, 7 minutes on foot. * Parking available. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Futondaiko|ja|布団太鼓}} * {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿}} == External Links == * [https://rokujinjya.jp/ Official Website] {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Kobe]] [[Category:History of Tarumi Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tarumi Ward]] [[Category:Daikokuten]] [[ja:舞子六神社]] [[Category:Maiko Roku Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:Maiko Roku Shrine]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Roku Shrine]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Mass bump]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:舞子六神社]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:舞子六神社]] [[ja:六神社]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Maiko Roku Shrine]][[simple:user:immanuelle/六神社]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

The shrine's deities are the same as those of the {{ill|Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|ja|岩屋神社 (明石市)}} in Akashi, suggesting that its foundation dates to the early Edo period, when spirits from Iwaya Shrine were enshrined here. The shrine served as the general {{ill|Chinju|ja|鎮守}} (village guardian) of Yamada Village in Harima Province. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) decree in the early Meiji era, the shrine's name changed from "Rokusha Daimyōjin" to "Rokusha Shrine" in 1874 (Meiji 7). It was later renamed "Roku Shrine," and the current main hall, rebuilt in 1890 (Meiji 23), was renovated with a copper roof between 1906 and 1907. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美大神}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞男大神}} * {{ill|Tsukuyomi|ja|月夜見大神}} * {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子大神}} == Sub-Shrines == * Ōtoshisha (豊宇気比賣命) * Shirahige-sha (Kifune Taisha) * Ebisu-miya (Hiruko Taisha) * Inari Omatsu-sha (Kurainatama Taisha) * Inari Gosha (Inari Ōkami) * Sugawara-sha (Sugawara Taisha) * Daikoku-miya ({{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}) == Festivals == The annual autumn festival is held on the second Sunday of October, with a preparatory night festival on the preceding Saturday. Festivities include mikoshi (portable shrines) and {{ill|Futondaiko|ja|布団太鼓}} processions. A traditional boat procession was previously part of the celebrations but is no longer conducted. Other festivals, such as Setsubun and summer festivals, are also held. === Futon Daiko === The original futon daiko was lost in a storage fire, leaving only mikoshi for the autumn festival for many years. The current futon daiko was acquired from {{ill|Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|ja|岩屋神社 (明石市)}}, which donated it after commissioning a new one. The "Futon Daiko Maiko Association" oversees its management and parade. == Access == * Nearest station: {{ill|Nishi-Maiko Station|ja|西舞子駅}} on the {{ill|Sanyo Electric Railway|ja|山陽電気鉄道}}, 3 minutes on foot; or {{ill|Maiko Station|ja|舞子駅}} on the JR line, 7 minutes on foot. * Parking available. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Futondaiko|ja|布団太鼓}} * {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿}} == External Links == * [https://rokujinjya.jp/ Official Website] {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Kobe]] [[Category:History of Tarumi Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tarumi Ward]] [[Category:Daikokuten]] [[ja:舞子六神社]] [[Category:Maiko Roku Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:Maiko Roku Shrine]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Roku Shrine]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Mass bump]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:舞子六神社]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:舞子六神社]] [[ja:六神社]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Maiko Roku Shrine]][[simple:user:immanuelle/六神社]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

{{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子大神}} | type = | consecration_year = | Main Hall = | Alternate names = | festival = Second Saturday and Sunday of October (weekend before Sports Day) | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Kobe#Japan Hyogo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=38|lat_sec=14|lon_deg=135|lon_min=1|lon_sec=44|label=Maiko Roku Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Maiko Roku Shrine''' (舞子六神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Tarumi Ward|ja|垂水区}}, {{ill|Kobe City|ja|神戸市}}, {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}. The shrine features large stone statues of {{ill|Ebisu (god)|ja|戎}} and {{ill|Daikokuten|ja|大黒天}}, which are said to be the largest in Japan. == History == The shrine's foundation can be traced back to {{ill|1689|ja|元禄}} (Genroku 2), as evidenced by inscriptions on the golden staff carried during the mikoshi procession. However, due to the loss of historical records, details prior to this period remain unclear. The shrine's deities are the same as those of the {{ill|Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|ja|岩屋神社 (明石市)}} in Akashi, suggesting that its foundation dates to the early Edo period, when spirits from Iwaya Shrine were enshrined here. The shrine served as the general {{ill|Chinju|ja|鎮守}} (village guardian) of Yamada Village in Harima Province. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) decree in the early Meiji era, the shrine's name changed from "Rokusha Daimyōjin" to "Rokusha Shrine" in 1874 (Meiji 7). It was later renamed "Roku Shrine," and the current main hall, rebuilt in 1890 (Meiji 23), was renovated with a copper roof between 1906 and 1907. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美大神}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞男大神}} * {{ill|Tsukuyomi|ja|月夜見大神}} * {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子大神}} == Sub-Shrines == * Ōtoshisha (豊宇気比賣命) * Shirahige-sha (Kifune Taisha) * Ebisu-miya (Hiruko Taisha) * Inari Omatsu-sha (Kurainatama Taisha) * Inari Gosha (Inari Ōkami) * Sugawara-sha (Sugawara Taisha) * Daikoku-miya ({{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}) == Festivals == The annual autumn festival is held on the second Sunday of October, with a preparatory night festival on the preceding Saturday. Festivities include mikoshi (portable shrines) and {{ill|Futondaiko|ja|布団太鼓}} processions. A traditional boat procession was previously part of the celebrations but is no longer conducted. Other festivals, such as Setsubun and summer festivals, are also held. === Futon Daiko === The original futon daiko was lost in a storage fire, leaving only mikoshi for the autumn festival for many years. The current futon daiko was acquired from {{ill|Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|ja|岩屋神社 (明石市)}}, which donated it after commissioning a new one. The "Futon Daiko Maiko Association" oversees its management and parade. == Access == * Nearest station: {{ill|Nishi-Maiko Station|ja|西舞子駅}} on the {{ill|Sanyo Electric Railway|ja|山陽電気鉄道}}, 3 minutes on foot; or {{ill|Maiko Station|ja|舞子駅}} on the JR line, 7 minutes on foot. * Parking available. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Futondaiko|ja|布団太鼓}} * {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿}} == External Links == * [https://rokujinjya.jp/ Official Website] {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Kobe]] [[Category:History of Tarumi Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tarumi Ward]] [[Category:Daikokuten]] [[ja:舞子六神社]] [[Category:Maiko Roku Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:Maiko Roku Shrine]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Roku Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Mass bump]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:舞子六神社]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[ja:舞子六神社]] [[ja:六神社]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Maiko Roku Shrine]][[simple:user:immanuelle/六神社]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

{{Shinto Shrine | name = Maiko Roku Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Maiko rokujinzya.JPG|225px]] | location = {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Kobe City|ja|神戸市}}, {{ill|Tarumi Ward|ja|垂水区}}, Nishi-Maiko 1-5-7 | coordinates = {{Coord|34|38|14|N|135|1|44|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=舞子六神社}} | deity = {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美大神}}
{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}}
{{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞男大神}}
{{ill|Tsukuyomi|ja|月夜見大神}}
{{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子大神}} | type = | consecration_year = | Main Hall = | alternate_name = | festival = Second Saturday and Sunday of October (weekend before Sports Day) | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Kobe#Japan Hyogo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=38|lat_sec=14|lon_deg=135|lon_min=1|lon_sec=44|label=Maiko Roku Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Maiko Roku Shrine''' (舞子六神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Tarumi Ward|ja|垂水区}}, {{ill|Kobe City|ja|神戸市}}, {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}. The shrine features large stone statues of {{ill|Ebisu (god)|ja|戎}} and {{ill|Daikokuten|ja|大黒天}}, which are said to be the largest in Japan. == History == The shrine's foundation can be traced back to {{ill|1689|ja|元禄}} (Genroku 2), as evidenced by inscriptions on the golden staff carried during the mikoshi procession. However, due to the loss of historical records, details prior to this period remain unclear. The shrine's deities are the same as those of the {{ill|Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|ja|岩屋神社 (明石市)}} in Akashi, suggesting that its foundation dates to the early Edo period, when spirits from Iwaya Shrine were enshrined here. The shrine served as the general {{ill|Chinju|ja|鎮守}} (village guardian) of Yamada Village in Harima Province. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) decree in the early Meiji era, the shrine's name changed from "Rokusha Daimyōjin" to "Rokusha Shrine" in 1874 (Meiji 7). It was later renamed "Roku Shrine," and the current main hall, rebuilt in 1890 (Meiji 23), was renovated with a copper roof between 1906 and 1907. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美大神}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞男大神}} * {{ill|Tsukuyomi|ja|月夜見大神}} * {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子大神}} == Sub-Shrines == * Ōtoshisha (豊宇気比賣命) * Shirahige-sha (Kifune Taisha) * Ebisu-miya (Hiruko Taisha) * Inari Omatsu-sha (Kurainatama Taisha) * Inari Gosha (Inari Ōkami) * Sugawara-sha (Sugawara Taisha) * Daikoku-miya ({{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}) == Festivals == The annual autumn festival is held on the second Sunday of October, with a preparatory night festival on the preceding Saturday. Festivities include mikoshi (portable shrines) and {{ill|Futondaiko|ja|布団太鼓}} processions. A traditional boat procession was previously part of the celebrations but is no longer conducted. Other festivals, such as Setsubun and summer festivals, are also held. === Futon Daiko === The original futon daiko was lost in a storage fire, leaving only mikoshi for the autumn festival for many years. The current futon daiko was acquired from {{ill|Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|ja|岩屋神社 (明石市)}}, which donated it after commissioning a new one. The "Futon Daiko Maiko Association" oversees its management and parade. == Access == * Nearest station: {{ill|Nishi-Maiko Station|ja|西舞子駅}} on the {{ill|Sanyo Electric Railway|ja|山陽電気鉄道}}, 3 minutes on foot; or {{ill|Maiko Station|ja|舞子駅}} on the JR line, 7 minutes on foot. * Parking available. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Futondaiko|ja|布団太鼓}} * {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿}} == External Links == * [https://rokujinjya.jp/ Official Website] {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Kobe]] [[Category:History of Tarumi Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tarumi Ward]] [[Category:Daikokuten]] [[ja:舞子六神社]] [[Category:Maiko Roku Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:Maiko Roku Shrine]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Roku Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Mass bump]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:舞子六神社]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[ja:舞子六神社]] [[ja:六神社]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Maiko Roku Shrine]][[simple:user:immanuelle/六神社]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

The "Futon Daiko Maiko Association" oversees its management and parade. == Access == * Nearest station: {{ill|Nishi-Maiko Station|ja|西舞子駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Sanyo Electric Railway|ja|山陽電気鉄道|12=draft}}, 3 minutes on foot; or {{ill|Maiko Station|ja|舞子駅|12=draft}} on the JR line, 7 minutes on foot. * Parking available. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Futondaiko|ja|布団太鼓|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿|12=draft}} == External Links == * [https://rokujinjya.jp/ Official Website] {{shinto-stub}} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Maiko Roku Shrine]][[simple:user:immanuelle/六神社]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kobe]] [[Category:History of Tarumi Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tarumi Ward]] [[Category:Daikokuten]] [[Category:Maiko Roku Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Roku Shrine]] [[Category:Mass bump]] [[Category:舞子六神社]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:舞子六神社]] [[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]] [[ja:六神社]]

{{Shinto Shrine | name = Maiko Roku Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Maiko rokujinzya.JPG|225px]] | location = {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kobe City|ja|神戸市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tarumi Ward|ja|垂水区|12=draft}}, Nishi-Maiko 1-5-7 | coordinates = {{Coord|34|38|14|N|135|1|44|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=舞子六神社}} | deity = {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神|12=draft}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美大神|12=draft}}
{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|12=draft}}
{{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞男大神|12=draft}}
{{ill|Tsukuyomi|ja|月夜見大神|12=draft}}
{{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子大神|12=draft}} | type = | consecration_year = | Main Hall = | alternate_name = | festival = Second Saturday and Sunday of October (weekend before Sports Day) | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Kobe#Japan Hyogo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=38|lat_sec=14|lon_deg=135|lon_min=1|lon_sec=44|label=Maiko Roku Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Maiko Roku Shrine''' (舞子六神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Tarumi Ward|ja|垂水区|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kobe City|ja|神戸市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|12=draft}}. The shrine features large stone statues of {{ill|Ebisu (god)|ja|戎|12=draft}} and {{ill|Daikokuten|ja|大黒天|12=draft}}, which are said to be the largest in Japan. == History == The shrine's foundation can be traced back to {{ill|1689|ja|元禄|12=draft}} (Genroku 2), as evidenced by inscriptions on the golden staff carried during the mikoshi procession. However, due to the loss of historical records, details prior to this period remain unclear. The shrine's deities are the same as those of the {{ill|Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|ja|岩屋神社 (明石市)|12=draft}} in Akashi, suggesting that its foundation dates to the early Edo period, when spirits from Iwaya Shrine were enshrined here. The shrine served as the general {{ill|Chinju|ja|鎮守|12=draft}} (village guardian) of Yamada Village in Harima Province. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離|12=draft}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) decree in the early Meiji era, the shrine's name changed from "Rokusha Daimyōjin" to "Rokusha Shrine" in 1874 (Meiji 7). It was later renamed "Roku Shrine," and the current main hall, rebuilt in 1890 (Meiji 23), was renovated with a copper roof between 1906 and 1907. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美大神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞男大神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tsukuyomi|ja|月夜見大神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子大神|12=draft}} == Sub-Shrines == * Ōtoshisha (豊宇気比賣命) * Shirahige-sha (Kifune Taisha) * Ebisu-miya (Hiruko Taisha) * Inari Omatsu-sha (Kurainatama Taisha) * Inari Gosha (Inari Ōkami) * Sugawara-sha (Sugawara Taisha) * Daikoku-miya ({{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=draft}}) == Festivals == The annual autumn festival is held on the second Sunday of October, with a preparatory night festival on the preceding Saturday. Festivities include mikoshi (portable shrines) and {{ill|Futondaiko|ja|布団太鼓|12=draft}} processions. A traditional boat procession was previously part of the celebrations but is no longer conducted. Other festivals, such as Setsubun and summer festivals, are also held. === Futon Daiko === The original futon daiko was lost in a storage fire, leaving only mikoshi for the autumn festival for many years. The current futon daiko was acquired from {{ill|Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|ja|岩屋神社 (明石市)|12=draft}}, which donated it after commissioning a new one. The "Futon Daiko Maiko Association" oversees its management and parade. == Access == * Nearest station: {{ill|Nishi-Maiko Station|ja|西舞子駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Sanyo Electric Railway|ja|山陽電気鉄道|12=draft}}, 3 minutes on foot; or {{ill|Maiko Station|ja|舞子駅|12=draft}} on the JR line, 7 minutes on foot. * Parking available. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Futondaiko|ja|布団太鼓|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿|12=draft}} == External Links == * [https://rokujinjya.jp/ Official Website] {{shinto-stub}} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Maiko Roku Shrine]][[simple:user:immanuelle/六神社]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kobe]] [[Category:History of Tarumi Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tarumi Ward]] [[Category:Daikokuten]] [[Category:Maiko Roku Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Roku Shrine]] [[Category:Mass bump]] [[Category:舞子六神社]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:舞子六神社]] [[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]] [[ja:六神社]]

{{ill|Tsukuyomi|ja|月夜見大神|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子大神|12=draft|12=draft}} | type = | consecration_year = | Main Hall = | alternate_name = | festival = Second Saturday and Sunday of October (weekend before Sports Day) | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Kobe#Japan Hyogo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=38|lat_sec=14|lon_deg=135|lon_min=1|lon_sec=44|label=Maiko Roku Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Maiko Roku Shrine''' (舞子六神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Tarumi Ward|ja|垂水区|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kobe City|ja|神戸市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|12=draft|12=draft}}. The shrine features large stone statues of {{ill|Ebisu (god)|ja|戎|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Daikokuten|ja|大黒天|12=draft|12=draft}}, which are said to be the largest in Japan. == History == The shrine's foundation can be traced back to {{ill|1689|ja|元禄|12=draft|12=draft}} (Genroku 2), as evidenced by inscriptions on the golden staff carried during the mikoshi procession. However, due to the loss of historical records, details prior to this period remain unclear. The shrine's deities are the same as those of the {{ill|Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|ja|岩屋神社 (明石市)|12=draft|12=draft}} in Akashi, suggesting that its foundation dates to the early Edo period, when spirits from Iwaya Shrine were enshrined here. The shrine served as the general {{ill|Chinju|ja|鎮守|12=draft|12=draft}} (village guardian) of Yamada Village in Harima Province. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離|12=draft|12=draft}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) decree in the early Meiji era, the shrine's name changed from "Rokusha Daimyōjin" to "Rokusha Shrine" in 1874 (Meiji 7). It was later renamed "Roku Shrine," and the current main hall, rebuilt in 1890 (Meiji 23), was renovated with a copper roof between 1906 and 1907. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美大神|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞男大神|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tsukuyomi|ja|月夜見大神|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子大神|12=draft|12=draft}} == Sub-Shrines == * Ōtoshisha (豊宇気比賣命) * Shirahige-sha (Kifune Taisha) * Ebisu-miya (Hiruko Taisha) * Inari Omatsu-sha (Kurainatama Taisha) * Inari Gosha (Inari Ōkami) * Sugawara-sha (Sugawara Taisha) * Daikoku-miya ({{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=draft|12=draft}}) == Festivals == The annual autumn festival is held on the second Sunday of October, with a preparatory night festival on the preceding Saturday. Festivities include mikoshi (portable shrines) and {{ill|Futondaiko|ja|布団太鼓|12=draft|12=draft}} processions. A traditional boat procession was previously part of the celebrations but is no longer conducted. Other festivals, such as Setsubun and summer festivals, are also held. === Futon Daiko === The original futon daiko was lost in a storage fire, leaving only mikoshi for the autumn festival for many years. The current futon daiko was acquired from {{ill|Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|ja|岩屋神社 (明石市)|12=draft|12=draft}}, which donated it after commissioning a new one. The "Futon Daiko Maiko Association" oversees its management and parade. == Access == * Nearest station: {{ill|Nishi-Maiko Station|ja|西舞子駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Sanyo Electric Railway|ja|山陽電気鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}}, 3 minutes on foot; or {{ill|Maiko Station|ja|舞子駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the JR line, 7 minutes on foot. * Parking available. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Futondaiko|ja|布団太鼓|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * [https://rokujinjya.jp/ Official Website] {{shinto-stub}} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Maiko Roku Shrine]][[simple:user:immanuelle/六神社]] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kobe]] [[Category:History of Tarumi Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tarumi Ward]] [[Category:Daikokuten]] [[Category:Maiko Roku Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Roku Shrine]] [[Category:Mass bump]] [[Category:舞子六神社]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:舞子六神社]] [[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]] [[ja:六神社]]

revision.comment: 66 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:舞子六神社 up to rev 103719612
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 舞子六神社 |画像 = [[File:Maiko rokujinzya.JPG|225px]] |所在地 = [[兵庫県]][[神戸市]][[垂水区]]西舞子1-5-7 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|38|14|N|135|1|44|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title|neme=舞子六神社}} |祭神 = [[伊邪那岐大神]]
[[伊邪那美大神]]
[[天照皇大神]]
[[素盞男大神]]
[[月夜見大神]]
[[蛭子大神]] |社格 = |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = 10月第二土、日曜日（体育の日の前の土日） |神事 = |地図={{Location map|Kobe#Japan Hyogo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=38|lat_sec=14|lon_deg=135|lon_min=1|lon_sec=44|label=舞子六神社|position=center|width=200|float=center}}}} '''舞子六神社'''（まいころくじんじゃ）は、[[兵庫県]][[神戸市]][[垂水区]]にある[[神社]]である。境内にある[[戎]]、[[大黒天|大黒]]社の[[石像]]は[[日本一]]とされている「[[日本一の一覧#仏教]]」。。 ==由緒== 創建は、御輿渡御に供奉する金幣の刻銘により、[[1689年]]（[[元禄]]2年）まで遡ることができるが、神社に関する古記録が失われているので、それ以前については不詳である。しかし、祭神は明石の[[岩屋神社 (明石市)|岩屋神社]]と同神であることから[[江戸時代]]前期中葉に、岩屋神社より分霊を受け、[[播州]][[明石郡]]山田村の総[[鎮守]]として六社大明神の社を建て、六柱の神を祀ったのが創始であると推定される。 [[明治]]初期の[[神仏分離|神仏分離令]]により明神号が廃止され、六社神社と名所が変わり、[[1874年]]（明治7年）2月に列し、六神社と改称し、現在に至っている。現在の本殿は[[1890年]]（明治23年）[[7月31日]]に再建され、明治39、40年に[[屋根|銅板茸]]の屋根に改修されたものである。 ==祭神== *[[イザナギ|伊邪那岐大神]] *[[イザナミ|伊邪那美大神]] *[[天照大神|天照皇大神]] *[[スサノオ|素盞男大神]] *[[ツクヨミ|月夜見大神]] *[[ヒルコ|蛭子大神]] ==境内社== *大歳社（豊宇気比賣命） *白髭社（貴船大社） *戎宮（蛭子大社） *稲荷お松社（倉稲魂大社） *稲荷五社（[[稲荷大神]]） *菅原社（菅原大社） *大黒宮（[[大国主命]]） == 祭事 == 秋の大祭は、毎年10月第二日曜日が本宮、前日土曜日が宵宮で神輿、布団太鼓の巡行が行われる。船渡御も行われていたが、近年は行われていない。他に節分際、夏祭りなども行われている。 === ふとん太鼓 === ふとん太鼓は収納庫の火災で焼失したため、長年、神輿だけで秋祭りを行なっていたが、明石の[[岩屋神社 (明石市)|岩屋神社]]がふとん太鼓を新調した際に、譲り受けた物を現在使用している。「布団太鼓・舞子会」が巡行や管理を行なっている。 == 交通 == *最寄り駅:[[山陽電気鉄道|山陽電鉄]][[西舞子駅]]より徒歩3分、JR[[舞子駅]]より徒歩7分 *駐車場あり == 関連項目 == * [[ふとん太鼓]] * [[神輿]] == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == * [https://rokujinjya.jp/ 公式WEBサイト] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:まいころくしんしや}} [[Category:神戸市の神社]] [[Category:垂水区の歴史]] [[Category:垂水区の建築物]] [[Category:大黒天]]

[[素盞男大神]]
[[月夜見大神]]
[[蛭子大神]] |社格 = |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = 10月第二土、日曜日（体育の日の前の土日） |神事 = |地図={{Location map|Kobe#Japan Hyogo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=38|lat_sec=14|lon_deg=135|lon_min=1|lon_sec=44|label=舞子六神社|position=center|width=200|float=center}}}} '''舞子六神社'''（まいころくじんじゃ）は、[[兵庫県]][[神戸市]][[垂水区]]にある[[神社]]である。境内にある[[戎]]、[[大黒天|大黒]]社の[[石像]]は[[日本一]]とされている「[[日本一の一覧#仏教]]」。。 ==由緒== 創建は、御輿渡御に供奉する金幣の刻銘により、[[1689年]]（[[元禄]]2年）まで遡ることができるが、神社に関する古記録が失われているので、それ以前については不詳である。しかし、祭神は明石の[[岩屋神社 (明石市)|岩屋神社]]と同神であることから[[江戸時代]]前期中葉に、岩屋神社より分霊を受け、[[播州]][[明石郡]]山田村の総[[鎮守]]として六社大明神の社を建て、六柱の神を祀ったのが創始であると推定される。 [[明治]]初期の[[神仏分離|神仏分離令]]により明神号が廃止され、六社神社と名所が変わり、[[1874年]]（明治7年）2月に列し、六神社と改称し、現在に至っている。現在の本殿は[[1890年]]（明治23年）[[7月31日]]に再建され、明治39、40年に[[屋根|銅板茸]]の屋根に改修されたものである。 ==祭神== *[[イザナギ|伊邪那岐大神]] *[[イザナミ|伊邪那美大神]] *[[天照大神|天照皇大神]] *[[スサノオ|素盞男大神]] *[[ツクヨミ|月夜見大神]] *[[ヒルコ|蛭子大神]] ==境内社== *大歳社（豊宇気比賣命） *白髭社（貴船大社） *戎宮（蛭子大社） *稲荷お松社（倉稲魂大社） *稲荷五社（[[稲荷大神]]） *菅原社（菅原大社） *大黒宮（[[大国主命]]） == 祭事 == 秋の大祭は、毎年10月第二日曜日が本宮、前日土曜日が宵宮で神輿、布団太鼓の巡行が行われる。船渡御も行われていたが、近年は行われていない。他に節分際、夏祭りなども行われている。 === ふとん太鼓 === ふとん太鼓は収納庫の火災で焼失したため、長年、神輿だけで秋祭りを行なっていたが、明石の[[岩屋神社 (明石市)|岩屋神社]]がふとん太鼓を新調した際に、譲り受けた物を現在使用している。「布団太鼓・舞子会」が巡行や管理を行なっている。 == 交通 == *最寄り駅:[[山陽電気鉄道|山陽電鉄]][[西舞子駅]]より徒歩3分、JR[[舞子駅]]より徒歩7分 *駐車場あり == 関連項目 == * [[ふとん太鼓]] * [[神輿]] == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == * [https://rokujinjya.jp/ 公式WEBサイト] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:まいころくしんしや}} [[Category:神戸市の神社]] [[Category:垂水区の歴史]] [[Category:垂水区の建築物]] [[Category:大黒天]]

{{Shinto Shrine | name = Maiko Roku Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Maiko rokujinzya.JPG|225px]] | location = {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Kobe City|ja|神戸市}}, {{ill|Tarumi Ward|ja|垂水区}}, Nishi-Maiko 1-5-7 | coordinates = {{Coord|34|38|14|N|135|1|44|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=舞子六神社}} | deity = {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美大神}}
{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}}
{{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞男大神}}
{{ill|Tsukuyomi|ja|月夜見大神}}
{{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子大神}} | type = | consecration_year = | Main Hall = | alternate_name = | festival = Second Saturday and Sunday of October (weekend before Sports Day) | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Kobe#Japan Hyogo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=38|lat_sec=14|lon_deg=135|lon_min=1|lon_sec=44|label=Maiko Roku Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Maiko Roku Shrine''' (舞子六神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Tarumi Ward|ja|垂水区}}, {{ill|Kobe City|ja|神戸市}}, {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}. The shrine features large stone statues of {{ill|Ebisu (god)|ja|戎}} and {{ill|Daikokuten|ja|大黒天}}, which are said to be the largest in Japan. == History == The shrine's foundation can be traced back to {{ill|1689|ja|元禄}} (Genroku 2), as evidenced by inscriptions on the golden staff carried during the mikoshi procession. However, due to the loss of historical records, details prior to this period remain unclear. The shrine's deities are the same as those of the {{ill|Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|ja|岩屋神社 (明石市)}} in Akashi, suggesting that its foundation dates to the early Edo period, when spirits from Iwaya Shrine were enshrined here. The shrine served as the general {{ill|Chinju|ja|鎮守}} (village guardian) of Yamada Village in Harima Province. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) decree in the early Meiji era, the shrine's name changed from "Rokusha Daimyōjin" to "Rokusha Shrine" in 1874 (Meiji 7). It was later renamed "Roku Shrine," and the current main hall, rebuilt in 1890 (Meiji 23), was renovated with a copper roof between 1906 and 1907. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美大神}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞男大神}} * {{ill|Tsukuyomi|ja|月夜見大神}} * {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子大神}} == Sub-Shrines == * Ōtoshisha (豊宇気比賣命) * Shirahige-sha (Kifune Taisha) * Ebisu-miya (Hiruko Taisha) * Inari Omatsu-sha (Kurainatama Taisha) * Inari Gosha (Inari Ōkami) * Sugawara-sha (Sugawara Taisha) * Daikoku-miya ({{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}) == Festivals == The annual autumn festival is held on the second Sunday of October, with a preparatory night festival on the preceding Saturday. Festivities include mikoshi (portable shrines) and {{ill|Futondaiko|ja|布団太鼓}} processions. A traditional boat procession was previously part of the celebrations but is no longer conducted. Other festivals, such as Setsubun and summer festivals, are also held. === Futon Daiko === The original futon daiko was lost in a storage fire, leaving only mikoshi for the autumn festival for many years. The current futon daiko was acquired from {{ill|Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|ja|岩屋神社 (明石市)}}, which donated it after commissioning a new one. The "Futon Daiko Maiko Association" oversees its management and parade. == Access == * Nearest station: {{ill|Nishi-Maiko Station|ja|西舞子駅}} on the {{ill|Sanyo Electric Railway|ja|山陽電気鉄道}}, 3 minutes on foot; or {{ill|Maiko Station|ja|舞子駅}} on the JR line, 7 minutes on foot. * Parking available. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Futondaiko|ja|布団太鼓}} * {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿}} == External Links == * [https://rokujinjya.jp/ Official Website] {{shinto-stub}} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Maiko Roku Shrine]][[simple:user:immanuelle/六神社]] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kobe]] [[Category:History of Tarumi Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tarumi Ward]] [[Category:Daikokuten]] [[Category:Maiko Roku Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Roku Shrine]] [[Category:舞子六神社]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:舞子六神社]] [[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]] [[ja:六神社]] {{translated page|ja|舞子六神社|version=103719612|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Festivities include mikoshi (portable shrines) and {{ill|Futondaiko|ja|布団太鼓|qq=draft|qq=draft}} processions. A traditional boat procession was previously part of the celebrations but is no longer conducted. Other festivals, such as Setsubun and summer festivals, are also held. === Futon Daiko === The original futon daiko was lost in a storage fire, leaving only mikoshi for the autumn festival for many years. The current futon daiko was acquired from {{ill|Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|ja|岩屋神社 (明石市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which donated it after commissioning a new one. The "Futon Daiko Maiko Association" oversees its management and parade. == Access == * Nearest station: {{ill|Nishi-Maiko Station|ja|西舞子駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Sanyo Electric Railway|ja|山陽電気鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, 3 minutes on foot; or {{ill|Maiko Station|ja|舞子駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the JR line, 7 minutes on foot. * Parking available. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Futondaiko|ja|布団太鼓|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [https://rokujinjya.jp/ Official Website] {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|舞子六神社|version=103719612|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kobe]] [[Category:History of Tarumi Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tarumi Ward]] [[Category:Daikokuten]] [[Category:Maiko Roku Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Roku Shrine]] }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Maiko Roku Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/六神社]] [[ja:舞子六神社]] [[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]] [[ja:六神社]]

{{Shinto Shrine | name = Maiko Roku Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Maiko rokujinzya.JPG|225px]] | location = {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kobe City|ja|神戸市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tarumi Ward|ja|垂水区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Nishi-Maiko 1-5-7 | coordinates = {{Coord|34|38|14|N|135|1|44|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=舞子六神社}} | deity = {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞男大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Tsukuyomi|ja|月夜見大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | type = | consecration_year = | Main Hall = | alternate_name = | festival = Second Saturday and Sunday of October (weekend before Sports Day) | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Kobe#Japan Hyogo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=38|lat_sec=14|lon_deg=135|lon_min=1|lon_sec=44|label=Maiko Roku Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Maiko Roku Shrine''' (舞子六神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Tarumi Ward|ja|垂水区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kobe City|ja|神戸市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The shrine features large stone statues of {{ill|Ebisu (god)|ja|戎|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Daikokuten|ja|大黒天|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which are said to be the largest in Japan. == History == The shrine's foundation can be traced back to {{ill|1689|ja|元禄|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Genroku 2), as evidenced by inscriptions on the golden staff carried during the mikoshi procession. However, due to the loss of historical records, details prior to this period remain unclear. The shrine's deities are the same as those of the {{ill|Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|ja|岩屋神社 (明石市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Akashi, suggesting that its foundation dates to the early Edo period, when spirits from Iwaya Shrine were enshrined here. The shrine served as the general {{ill|Chinju|ja|鎮守|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (village guardian) of Yamada Village in Harima Province. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) decree in the early Meiji era, the shrine's name changed from "Rokusha Daimyōjin" to "Rokusha Shrine" in 1874 (Meiji 7). It was later renamed "Roku Shrine," and the current main hall, rebuilt in 1890 (Meiji 23), was renovated with a copper roof between 1906 and 1907. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞男大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsukuyomi|ja|月夜見大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == Sub-Shrines == * Ōtoshisha (豊宇気比賣命) * Shirahige-sha (Kifune Taisha) * Ebisu-miya (Hiruko Taisha) * Inari Omatsu-sha (Kurainatama Taisha) * Inari Gosha (Inari Ōkami) * Sugawara-sha (Sugawara Taisha) * Daikoku-miya ({{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) == Festivals == The annual autumn festival is held on the second Sunday of October, with a preparatory night festival on the preceding Saturday. Festivities include mikoshi (portable shrines) and {{ill|Futondaiko|ja|布団太鼓|qq=draft|qq=draft}} processions. A traditional boat procession was previously part of the celebrations but is no longer conducted. Other festivals, such as Setsubun and summer festivals, are also held. === Futon Daiko === The original futon daiko was lost in a storage fire, leaving only mikoshi for the autumn festival for many years. The current futon daiko was acquired from {{ill|Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|ja|岩屋神社 (明石市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which donated it after commissioning a new one. The "Futon Daiko Maiko Association" oversees its management and parade. == Access == * Nearest station: {{ill|Nishi-Maiko Station|ja|西舞子駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Sanyo Electric Railway|ja|山陽電気鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, 3 minutes on foot; or {{ill|Maiko Station|ja|舞子駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the JR line, 7 minutes on foot. * Parking available. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Futondaiko|ja|布団太鼓|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [https://rokujinjya.jp/ Official Website] {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|舞子六神社|version=103719612|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kobe]] [[Category:History of Tarumi Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tarumi Ward]] [[Category:Daikokuten]] [[Category:Maiko Roku Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Roku Shrine]] }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Maiko Roku Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/六神社]] [[ja:舞子六神社]] [[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]] [[ja:六神社]]

{{ill|Tsukuyomi|ja|月夜見大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi|qq=}}
{{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|qq=}} | type = | consecration_year = | Main Hall = | alternate_name = | festival = Second Saturday and Sunday of October (weekend before Sports Day) | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Kobe#Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=38|lat_sec=14|lon_deg=135|lon_min=1|lon_sec=44|label=Maiko Roku Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Maiko Roku Shrine''' (舞子六神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Tarumi Ward|ja|垂水区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarumi Ward|qq=}}, {{ill|Kobe City|ja|神戸市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe City|qq=}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefecture|qq=}}. The shrine features large stone statues of {{ill|Ebisu (god)|ja|戎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu (god)|qq=}} and {{ill|Daikokuten|ja|大黒天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daikokuten|qq=}}, which are said to be the largest in Japan. == History == The shrine's foundation can be traced back to {{ill|1689|ja|元禄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1689|qq=}} (Genroku 2), as evidenced by inscriptions on the golden staff carried during the mikoshi procession. However, due to the loss of historical records, details prior to this period remain unclear. The shrine's deities are the same as those of the {{ill|Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|ja|岩屋神社 (明石市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|qq=}} in Akashi, suggesting that its foundation dates to the early Edo period, when spirits from Iwaya Shrine were enshrined here. The shrine served as the general {{ill|Chinju|ja|鎮守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinju|qq=}} (village guardian) of Yamada Village in Harima Province. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|qq=}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) decree in the early Meiji era, the shrine's name changed from "Rokusha Daimyōjin" to "Rokusha Shrine" in 1874 (Meiji 7). It was later renamed "Roku Shrine," and the current main hall, rebuilt in 1890 (Meiji 23), was renovated with a copper roof between 1906 and 1907. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞男大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsukuyomi|ja|月夜見大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi|qq=}} * {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|qq=}} == Sub-Shrines == * Ōtoshisha (豊宇気比賣命) * Shirahige-sha (Kifune Taisha) * Ebisu-miya (Hiruko Taisha) * Inari Omatsu-sha (Kurainatama Taisha) * Inari Gosha (Inari Ōkami) * Sugawara-sha (Sugawara Taisha) * Daikoku-miya ({{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}) == Festivals == The annual autumn festival is held on the second Sunday of October, with a preparatory night festival on the preceding Saturday. Festivities include mikoshi (portable shrines) and {{ill|Futondaiko|ja|布団太鼓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futondaiko|qq=}} processions. A traditional boat procession was previously part of the celebrations but is no longer conducted. Other festivals, such as Setsubun and summer festivals, are also held. === Futon Daiko === The original futon daiko was lost in a storage fire, leaving only mikoshi for the autumn festival for many years. The current futon daiko was acquired from {{ill|Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|ja|岩屋神社 (明石市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|qq=}}, which donated it after commissioning a new one. The "Futon Daiko Maiko Association" oversees its management and parade. == Access == * Nearest station: {{ill|Nishi-Maiko Station|ja|西舞子駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-Maiko Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Sanyo Electric Railway|ja|山陽電気鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanyo Electric Railway|qq=}}, 3 minutes on foot; or {{ill|Maiko Station|ja|舞子駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maiko Station|qq=}} on the JR line, 7 minutes on foot. * Parking available. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Futondaiko|ja|布団太鼓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futondaiko|qq=}} * {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikoshi|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://rokujinjya.jp/ Official Website] {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|舞子六神社|version=103719612|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kobe]] [[Category:History of Tarumi Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tarumi Ward]] [[Category:Daikokuten]] [[Category:Maiko Roku Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Roku Shrine]] }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Maiko Roku Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/六神社]] [[ja:舞子六神社]] [[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]] [[ja:六神社]]

{{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|qq=}} | type = | consecration_year = | Main Hall = | alternate_name = | festival = Second Saturday and Sunday of October (weekend before Sports Day) | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Kobe#Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=38|lat_sec=14|lon_deg=135|lon_min=1|lon_sec=44|label=Maiko Roku Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Maiko Roku Shrine''' (舞子六神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Tarumi Ward|ja|垂水区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarumi Ward|qq=}}, {{ill|Kobe City|ja|神戸市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe City|qq=}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefecture|qq=}}. The shrine features large stone statues of {{ill|Ebisu (god)|ja|戎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu (god)|qq=}} and {{ill|Daikokuten|ja|大黒天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daikokuten|qq=}}, which are said to be the largest in Japan. == History == The shrine's foundation can be traced back to {{ill|1689|ja|元禄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1689|qq=}} (Genroku 2), as evidenced by inscriptions on the golden staff carried during the mikoshi procession. However, due to the loss of historical records, details prior to this period remain unclear. The shrine's deities are the same as those of the {{ill|Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|ja|岩屋神社 (明石市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|qq=}} in Akashi, suggesting that its foundation dates to the early Edo period, when spirits from Iwaya Shrine were enshrined here. The shrine served as the general {{ill|Chinju|ja|鎮守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinju|qq=}} (village guardian) of Yamada Village in Harima Province. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|qq=}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) decree in the early Meiji era, the shrine's name changed from "Rokusha Daimyōjin" to "Rokusha Shrine" in 1874 (Meiji 7). It was later renamed "Roku Shrine," and the current main hall, rebuilt in 1890 (Meiji 23), was renovated with a copper roof between 1906 and 1907. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞男大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsukuyomi|ja|月夜見大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi|qq=}} * {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|qq=}} == Sub-Shrines == * Ōtoshisha (豊宇気比賣命) * Shirahige-sha (Kifune Taisha) * Ebisu-miya (Hiruko Taisha) * Inari Omatsu-sha (Kurainatama Taisha) * Inari Gosha (Inari Ōkami) * Sugawara-sha (Sugawara Taisha) * Daikoku-miya ({{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}) == Festivals == The annual autumn festival is held on the second Sunday of October, with a preparatory night festival on the preceding Saturday. Festivities include mikoshi (portable shrines) and {{ill|Futondaiko|ja|布団太鼓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futondaiko|qq=}} processions. A traditional boat procession was previously part of the celebrations but is no longer conducted. Other festivals, such as Setsubun and summer festivals, are also held. === Futon Daiko === The original futon daiko was lost in a storage fire, leaving only mikoshi for the autumn festival for many years. The current futon daiko was acquired from {{ill|Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|ja|岩屋神社 (明石市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|qq=}}, which donated it after commissioning a new one. The "Futon Daiko Maiko Association" oversees its management and parade. == Access == * Nearest station: {{ill|Nishi-Maiko Station|ja|西舞子駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-Maiko Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Sanyo Electric Railway|ja|山陽電気鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanyo Electric Railway|qq=}}, 3 minutes on foot; or {{ill|Maiko Station|ja|舞子駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maiko Station|qq=}} on the JR line, 7 minutes on foot. * Parking available. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Futondaiko|ja|布団太鼓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futondaiko|qq=}} * {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikoshi|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://rokujinjya.jp/ Official Website] {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|舞子六神社|version=103719612|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kobe]] [[Category:History of Tarumi Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tarumi Ward]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Maiko Roku Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/六神社]] [[ja:舞子六神社]] [[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]] [[ja:六神社]]

It was later renamed "Roku Shrine," and the current main hall, rebuilt in 1890 (Meiji 23), was renovated with a copper roof between 1906 and 1907. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞男大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tsukuyomi|ja|月夜見大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi|qq=}} * {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|qq=}} == Sub-Shrines == * Ōtoshisha (豊宇気比賣命) * Shirahige-sha (Kifune Taisha) * Ebisu-miya (Hiruko Taisha) * Inari Omatsu-sha (Kurainatama Taisha) * Inari Gosha (Inari Ōkami) * Sugawara-sha (Sugawara Taisha) * Daikoku-miya ({{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}) == Festivals == The annual autumn festival is held on the second Sunday of October, with a preparatory night festival on the preceding Saturday. Festivities include mikoshi (portable shrines) and {{ill|Futondaiko|ja|布団太鼓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futondaiko|qq=}} processions. A traditional boat procession was previously part of the celebrations but is no longer conducted. Other festivals, such as Setsubun and summer festivals, are also held. === Futon Daiko === The original futon daiko was lost in a storage fire, leaving only mikoshi for the autumn festival for many years. The current futon daiko was acquired from {{ill|Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|ja|岩屋神社 (明石市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|qq=}}, which donated it after commissioning a new one. The "Futon Daiko Maiko Association" oversees its management and parade. == Access == * Nearest station: [[:en:Nishi-Maiko Station|Nishi-Maiko Station]] on the [[:en:Sanyo Electric Railway|Sanyo Electric Railway]], 3 minutes on foot; or [[:en:Maiko Station|Maiko Station]] on the JR line, 7 minutes on foot. * Parking available. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Futondaiko|ja|布団太鼓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futondaiko|qq=}} * [[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]] == External Links == * [https://rokujinjya.jp/ Official Website] {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|舞子六神社|version=103719612|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kobe]] [[Category:History of Tarumi Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tarumi Ward]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Maiko Roku Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/六神社]] [[ja:舞子六神社]] [[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]] [[ja:六神社]] [[Category:垂水区の建築物]]

{{Shinto Shrine | name = Maiko Roku Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Maiko rokujinzya.JPG|225px]] | location = [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[:en:Kobe|Kobe City]], [[:en:Tarumi-ku, Kobe|Tarumi Ward]], Nishi-Maiko 1-5-7 | coordinates = {{Coord|34|38|14|N|135|1|44|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=舞子六神社}} | deity = {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=}}
{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}
{{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞男大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tsukuyomi|ja|月夜見大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi|qq=}}
{{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|qq=}} | type = | consecration_year = | Main Hall = | alternate_name = | festival = Second Saturday and Sunday of October (weekend before Sports Day) | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Kobe#Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=38|lat_sec=14|lon_deg=135|lon_min=1|lon_sec=44|label=Maiko Roku Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Maiko Roku Shrine''' (舞子六神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tarumi-ku, Kobe|Tarumi Ward]], [[:en:Kobe|Kobe City]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. The shrine features large stone statues of {{ill|Ebisu (god)|ja|戎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu (god)|qq=}} and [[:en:Daikokuten|Daikokuten]], which are said to be the largest in Japan. == History == The shrine's foundation can be traced back to [[:en:Genroku|1689]] (Genroku 2), as evidenced by inscriptions on the golden staff carried during the mikoshi procession. However, due to the loss of historical records, details prior to this period remain unclear. The shrine's deities are the same as those of the {{ill|Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|ja|岩屋神社 (明石市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|qq=}} in Akashi, suggesting that its foundation dates to the early Edo period, when spirits from Iwaya Shrine were enshrined here. The shrine served as the general {{ill|Chinju|ja|鎮守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinju|qq=}} (village guardian) of Yamada Village in Harima Province. Following the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) decree in the early Meiji era, the shrine's name changed from "Rokusha Daimyōjin" to "Rokusha Shrine" in 1874 (Meiji 7). It was later renamed "Roku Shrine," and the current main hall, rebuilt in 1890 (Meiji 23), was renovated with a copper roof between 1906 and 1907. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞男大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tsukuyomi|ja|月夜見大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi|qq=}} * {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|qq=}} == Sub-Shrines == * Ōtoshisha (豊宇気比賣命) * Shirahige-sha (Kifune Taisha) * Ebisu-miya (Hiruko Taisha) * Inari Omatsu-sha (Kurainatama Taisha) * Inari Gosha (Inari Ōkami) * Sugawara-sha (Sugawara Taisha) * Daikoku-miya ({{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}) == Festivals == The annual autumn festival is held on the second Sunday of October, with a preparatory night festival on the preceding Saturday. Festivities include mikoshi (portable shrines) and {{ill|Futondaiko|ja|布団太鼓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futondaiko|qq=}} processions. A traditional boat procession was previously part of the celebrations but is no longer conducted. Other festivals, such as Setsubun and summer festivals, are also held. === Futon Daiko === The original futon daiko was lost in a storage fire, leaving only mikoshi for the autumn festival for many years. The current futon daiko was acquired from {{ill|Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|ja|岩屋神社 (明石市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|qq=}}, which donated it after commissioning a new one. The "Futon Daiko Maiko Association" oversees its management and parade. == Access == * Nearest station: [[:en:Nishi-Maiko Station|Nishi-Maiko Station]] on the [[:en:Sanyo Electric Railway|Sanyo Electric Railway]], 3 minutes on foot; or [[:en:Maiko Station|Maiko Station]] on the JR line, 7 minutes on foot. * Parking available. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Futondaiko|ja|布団太鼓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futondaiko|qq=}} * [[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]] == External Links == * [https://rokujinjya.jp/ Official Website] {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|舞子六神社|version=103719612|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kobe]] [[Category:History of Tarumi Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tarumi Ward]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Maiko Roku Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/六神社]] [[ja:舞子六神社]] [[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]] [[ja:六神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Tarumi-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:垂水区の歴史]] [[Category:大黒天]] [[Category:神戸市の神社]]

It was later renamed "Roku Shrine," and the current main hall, rebuilt in 1890 (Meiji 23), was renovated with a copper roof between 1906 and 1907. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞男大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tsukuyomi|ja|月夜見大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi|qq=}} * {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|qq=}} == Sub-Shrines == * Ōtoshisha (豊宇気比賣命) * Shirahige-sha (Kifune Taisha) * Ebisu-miya (Hiruko Taisha) * Inari Omatsu-sha (Kurainatama Taisha) * Inari Gosha (Inari Ōkami) * Sugawara-sha (Sugawara Taisha) * Daikoku-miya ({{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}) == Festivals == The annual autumn festival is held on the second Sunday of October, with a preparatory night festival on the preceding Saturday. Festivities include mikoshi (portable shrines) and {{ill|Futondaiko|ja|布団太鼓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futondaiko|qq=}} processions. A traditional boat procession was previously part of the celebrations but is no longer conducted. Other festivals, such as Setsubun and summer festivals, are also held. === Futon Daiko === The original futon daiko was lost in a storage fire, leaving only mikoshi for the autumn festival for many years. The current futon daiko was acquired from {{ill|Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|ja|岩屋神社 (明石市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|qq=}}, which donated it after commissioning a new one. The "Futon Daiko Maiko Association" oversees its management and parade. == Access == * Nearest station: [[:en:Nishi-Maiko Station|Nishi-Maiko Station]] on the [[:en:Sanyo Electric Railway|Sanyo Electric Railway]], 3 minutes on foot; or [[:en:Maiko Station|Maiko Station]] on the JR line, 7 minutes on foot. * Parking available. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Futondaiko|ja|布団太鼓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futondaiko|qq=}} * [[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]] == External Links == * [https://rokujinjya.jp/ Official Website] {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|舞子六神社|version=103719612|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kobe]] [[Category:History of Tarumi Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tarumi Ward]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Maiko Roku Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/六神社]] [[ja:舞子六神社]] [[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]] [[ja:六神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Tarumi-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:History of Tarumi-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:Daikokuten�]] [[Category:神戸市の神社]]

{{Shinto Shrine | name = Maiko Roku Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Maiko rokujinzya.JPG|225px]] | location = [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[:en:Kobe|Kobe City]], [[:en:Tarumi-ku, Kobe|Tarumi Ward]], Nishi-Maiko 1-5-7 | coordinates = {{Coord|34|38|14|N|135|1|44|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=舞子六神社}} | deity = {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=}}
{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}
{{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞男大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tsukuyomi|ja|月夜見大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi|qq=}}
{{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|qq=}} | type = | consecration_year = | Main Hall = | alternate_name = | festival = Second Saturday and Sunday of October (weekend before Sports Day) | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Kobe#Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=38|lat_sec=14|lon_deg=135|lon_min=1|lon_sec=44|label=Maiko Roku Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Maiko Roku Shrine''' (舞子六神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tarumi-ku, Kobe|Tarumi Ward]], [[:en:Kobe|Kobe City]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. The shrine features large stone statues of {{ill|Ebisu (god)|ja|戎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu (god)|qq=}} and [[:en:Daikokuten|Daikokuten]], which are said to be the largest in Japan. == History == The shrine's foundation can be traced back to [[:en:Genroku|1689]] (Genroku 2), as evidenced by inscriptions on the golden staff carried during the mikoshi procession. However, due to the loss of historical records, details prior to this period remain unclear. The shrine's deities are the same as those of the {{ill|Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|ja|岩屋神社 (明石市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|qq=}} in Akashi, suggesting that its foundation dates to the early Edo period, when spirits from Iwaya Shrine were enshrined here. The shrine served as the general {{ill|Chinju|ja|鎮守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinju|qq=}} (village guardian) of Yamada Village in Harima Province. Following the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) decree in the early Meiji era, the shrine's name changed from "Rokusha Daimyōjin" to "Rokusha Shrine" in 1874 (Meiji 7). It was later renamed "Roku Shrine," and the current main hall, rebuilt in 1890 (Meiji 23), was renovated with a copper roof between 1906 and 1907. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞男大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tsukuyomi|ja|月夜見大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi|qq=}} * {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|qq=}} == Sub-Shrines == * Ōtoshisha (豊宇気比賣命) * Shirahige-sha (Kifune Taisha) * Ebisu-miya (Hiruko Taisha) * Inari Omatsu-sha (Kurainatama Taisha) * Inari Gosha (Inari Ōkami) * Sugawara-sha (Sugawara Taisha) * Daikoku-miya ({{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}) == Festivals == The annual autumn festival is held on the second Sunday of October, with a preparatory night festival on the preceding Saturday. Festivities include mikoshi (portable shrines) and {{ill|Futondaiko|ja|布団太鼓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futondaiko|qq=}} processions. A traditional boat procession was previously part of the celebrations but is no longer conducted. Other festivals, such as Setsubun and summer festivals, are also held. === Futon Daiko === The original futon daiko was lost in a storage fire, leaving only mikoshi for the autumn festival for many years. The current futon daiko was acquired from {{ill|Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|ja|岩屋神社 (明石市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|qq=}}, which donated it after commissioning a new one. The "Futon Daiko Maiko Association" oversees its management and parade. == Access == * Nearest station: [[:en:Nishi-Maiko Station|Nishi-Maiko Station]] on the [[:en:Sanyo Electric Railway|Sanyo Electric Railway]], 3 minutes on foot; or [[:en:Maiko Station|Maiko Station]] on the JR line, 7 minutes on foot. * Parking available. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Futondaiko|ja|布団太鼓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futondaiko|qq=}} * [[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]] == External Links == * [https://rokujinjya.jp/ Official Website] {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|舞子六神社|version=103719612|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kobe]] [[Category:History of Tarumi Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tarumi Ward]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Maiko Roku Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/六神社]] [[ja:舞子六神社]] [[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]] [[ja:六神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Tarumi-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:History of Tarumi-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:Daikokuten�]] [[Category:Shrines in Kobe�]]

The "Futon Daiko Maiko Association" oversees its management and parade. == Access == * Nearest station: [[:en:Nishi-Maiko Station|Nishi-Maiko Station]] on the [[:en:Sanyo Electric Railway|Sanyo Electric Railway]], 3 minutes on foot; or [[:en:Maiko Station|Maiko Station]] on the JR line, 7 minutes on foot. * Parking available. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Futondaiko|ja|布団太鼓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futondaiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ふとん太鼓}} * [[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]] == External Links == * [https://rokujinjya.jp/ Official Website] {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|舞子六神社|version=103719612|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kobe]] [[Category:History of Tarumi Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tarumi Ward]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Maiko Roku Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/六神社]] [[ja:舞子六神社]] [[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]] [[ja:六神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Tarumi-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:History of Tarumi-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:Daikokuten]] [[Category:Shrines in Kobe]]

revision.text:
{{Shinto Shrine | name = Maiko Roku Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Maiko rokujinzya.JPG|225px]] | location = [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[:en:Kobe|Kobe City]], [[:en:Tarumi-ku, Kobe|Tarumi Ward]], Nishi-Maiko 1-5-7 | coordinates = {{Coord|34|38|14|N|135|1|44|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=舞子六神社}} | deity = {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ}}
{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}
{{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞男大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
{{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tsukuyomi|ja|月夜見大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ}}
{{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to えびす}} | type = | consecration_year = | Main Hall = | alternate_name = | festival = Second Saturday and Sunday of October (weekend before Sports Day) | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Kobe#Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=38|lat_sec=14|lon_deg=135|lon_min=1|lon_sec=44|label=Maiko Roku Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Maiko Roku Shrine''' (舞子六神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tarumi-ku, Kobe|Tarumi Ward]], [[:en:Kobe|Kobe City]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. The shrine features large stone statues of {{ill|Ebisu (god)|ja|戎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu (god)|qq=}} and [[:en:Daikokuten|Daikokuten]], which are said to be the largest in Japan. == History == The shrine's foundation can be traced back to [[:en:Genroku|1689]] (Genroku 2), as evidenced by inscriptions on the golden staff carried during the mikoshi procession. However, due to the loss of historical records, details prior to this period remain unclear. The shrine's deities are the same as those of the {{ill|Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|ja|岩屋神社 (明石市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|qq=}} in Akashi, suggesting that its foundation dates to the early Edo period, when spirits from Iwaya Shrine were enshrined here. The shrine served as the general {{ill|Chinju|ja|鎮守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinju|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎮守神}} (village guardian) of Yamada Village in Harima Province. Following the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) decree in the early Meiji era, the shrine's name changed from "Rokusha Daimyōjin" to "Rokusha Shrine" in 1874 (Meiji 7). It was later renamed "Roku Shrine," and the current main hall, rebuilt in 1890 (Meiji 23), was renovated with a copper roof between 1906 and 1907. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞男大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tsukuyomi|ja|月夜見大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ}} * {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to えびす}} == Sub-Shrines == * Ōtoshisha (豊宇気比賣命) * Shirahige-sha (Kifune Taisha) * Ebisu-miya (Hiruko Taisha) * Inari Omatsu-sha (Kurainatama Taisha) * Inari Gosha (Inari Ōkami) * Sugawara-sha (Sugawara Taisha) * Daikoku-miya ({{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}) == Festivals == The annual autumn festival is held on the second Sunday of October, with a preparatory night festival on the preceding Saturday. Festivities include mikoshi (portable shrines) and {{ill|Futondaiko|ja|布団太鼓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futondaiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ふとん太鼓}} processions. A traditional boat procession was previously part of the celebrations but is no longer conducted. Other festivals, such as Setsubun and summer festivals, are also held. === Futon Daiko === The original futon daiko was lost in a storage fire, leaving only mikoshi for the autumn festival for many years. The current futon daiko was acquired from {{ill|Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|ja|岩屋神社 (明石市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|qq=}}, which donated it after commissioning a new one. The "Futon Daiko Maiko Association" oversees its management and parade. == Access == * Nearest station: [[:en:Nishi-Maiko Station|Nishi-Maiko Station]] on the [[:en:Sanyo Electric Railway|Sanyo Electric Railway]], 3 minutes on foot; or [[:en:Maiko Station|Maiko Station]] on the JR line, 7 minutes on foot. * Parking available. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Futondaiko|ja|布団太鼓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futondaiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ふとん太鼓}} * [[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]] == External Links == * [https://rokujinjya.jp/ Official Website] {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|舞子六神社|version=103719612|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kobe]] [[Category:History of Tarumi Ward]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Maiko Roku Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/六神社]] [[ja:舞子六神社]] [[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]] [[ja:六神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Tarumi-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:History of Tarumi-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:Daikokuten]] [[Category:Shrines in Kobe]]
