{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11613612]] Wikidata name: Maikoroku-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Shinto Shrine | name = Maiko Roku Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Maiko rokujinzya.JPG|225px]] | location = [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[:en:Kobe|Kobe City]], [[:en:Tarumi-ku, Kobe|Tarumi Ward]], Nishi-Maiko 1-5-7 | coordinates = {{Coord|34|38|14|N|135|1|44|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=舞子六神社}} | deity = {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ}}
{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}
{{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞男大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
{{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tsukuyomi|ja|月夜見大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ}}
{{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to えびす}} | type = | consecration_year = | Main Hall = | alternate_name = | festival = Second Saturday and Sunday of October (weekend before Sports Day) | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Kobe#Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=38|lat_sec=14|lon_deg=135|lon_min=1|lon_sec=44|label=Maiko Roku Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Maiko Roku Shrine''' (舞子六神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tarumi-ku, Kobe|Tarumi Ward]], [[:en:Kobe|Kobe City]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. The shrine features large stone statues of {{ill|Ebisu (god)|ja|戎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu (god)|qq=}} and [[:en:Daikokuten|Daikokuten]], which are said to be the largest in Japan. == History == The shrine's foundation can be traced back to [[:en:Genroku|1689]] (Genroku 2), as evidenced by inscriptions on the golden staff carried during the mikoshi procession. However, due to the loss of historical records, details prior to this period remain unclear. The shrine's deities are the same as those of the {{ill|Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|ja|岩屋神社 (明石市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|qq=}} in Akashi, suggesting that its foundation dates to the early Edo period, when spirits from Iwaya Shrine were enshrined here. The shrine served as the general {{ill|Chinju|ja|鎮守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinju|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎮守神}} (village guardian) of Yamada Village in Harima Province. Following the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) decree in the early Meiji era, the shrine's name changed from "Rokusha Daimyōjin" to "Rokusha Shrine" in 1874 (Meiji 7). It was later renamed "Roku Shrine," and the current main hall, rebuilt in 1890 (Meiji 23), was renovated with a copper roof between 1906 and 1907. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞男大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tsukuyomi|ja|月夜見大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ}} * {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to えびす}} == Sub-Shrines == * Ōtoshisha (豊宇気比賣命) * Shirahige-sha (Kifune Taisha) * Ebisu-miya (Hiruko Taisha) * Inari Omatsu-sha (Kurainatama Taisha) * Inari Gosha (Inari Ōkami) * Sugawara-sha (Sugawara Taisha) * Daikoku-miya ({{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}) == Festivals == The annual autumn festival is held on the second Sunday of October, with a preparatory night festival on the preceding Saturday. Festivities include mikoshi (portable shrines) and {{ill|Futondaiko|ja|布団太鼓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futondaiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ふとん太鼓}} processions. A traditional boat procession was previously part of the celebrations but is no longer conducted. Other festivals, such as Setsubun and summer festivals, are also held. === Futon Daiko === The original futon daiko was lost in a storage fire, leaving only mikoshi for the autumn festival for many years. The current futon daiko was acquired from {{ill|Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|ja|岩屋神社 (明石市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|qq=}}, which donated it after commissioning a new one. The "Futon Daiko Maiko Association" oversees its management and parade. == Access == * Nearest station: [[:en:Nishi-Maiko Station|Nishi-Maiko Station]] on the [[:en:Sanyo Electric Railway|Sanyo Electric Railway]], 3 minutes on foot; or [[:en:Maiko Station|Maiko Station]] on the JR line, 7 minutes on foot. * Parking available. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Futondaiko|ja|布団太鼓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futondaiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ふとん太鼓}} * [[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]] == External Links == * [https://rokujinjya.jp/ Official Website] {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|舞子六神社|version=103719612|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kobe]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Maiko Roku Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/六神社]] [[ja:舞子六神社]] [[sn:Maiko Roku Shrine]] [[ja:六神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Tarumi-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:History of Tarumi-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:Daikokuten]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kobe]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:舞子六神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11613612]] Wikidata name: Maikoroku-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| | name = Maiko Roku Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Maiko rokujinzya.JPG | location = [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[:en:Kobe|Kobe City]], [[:en:Tarumi-ku, Kobe|Tarumi Ward]], Nishi-Maiko 1-5-7 | coordinates = {{Coord|34.637222|N|135.028889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602}}; {{ill|Hiruko|qid=Q1067028}}; {{ill|Izanagi|qid=Q813858}}; {{ill|Izanami|qid=Q682306}}; {{ill|Susanoo|qid=Q272993}}; {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|qid=Q595520}} | type = | consecration_year = | Main Hall = | alternate_name = | festival = Second Saturday and Sunday of October (weekend before Sports Day) | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Kobe#Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=38|lat_sec=14|lon_deg=135|lon_min=1|lon_sec=44|label=Maiko Roku Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} | location = {{ill|Tarumi-ku|qid=Q1078806}} }}
'''Maiko Roku Shrine''' (舞子六神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tarumi-ku, Kobe|Tarumi Ward]], [[:en:Kobe|Kobe City]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. The shrine features large stone statues of {{ill|Ebisu (god)|ja|戎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu (god)|qq=|1=Ebisu (god)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495340}} and [[:en:Daikokuten|Daikokuten]], which are said to be the largest in Japan.
== History == The shrine's foundation can be traced back to [[:en:Genroku|1689]] (Genroku 2), as evidenced by inscriptions on the golden staff carried during the mikoshi procession. However, due to the loss of historical records, details prior to this period remain unclear. The shrine's deities are the same as those of the {{ill|Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|ja|岩屋神社 (明石市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|qq=|1=Iwaya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11474394}} in Akashi, suggesting that its foundation dates to the early Edo period, when spirits from Iwaya Shrine were enshrined here. The shrine served as the general {{ill|Chinju|ja|鎮守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinju|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎮守神|1=chinjugami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97479976}} (village guardian) of Yamada Village in Harima Province.
Following the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) decree in the early Meiji era, the shrine's name changed from "Rokusha Daimyōjin" to "Rokusha Shrine" in 1874 (Meiji 7). It was later renamed "Roku Shrine," and the current main hall, rebuilt in 1890 (Meiji 23), was renovated with a copper roof between 1906 and 1907.
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞男大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tsukuyomi|ja|月夜見大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ}} * {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to えびす|1=Ebisu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1129330}}
== Sub-Shrines == * Ōtoshisha (豊宇気比賣命) * Shirahige-sha (Kifune Taisha) * Ebisu-miya (Hiruko Taisha) * Inari Omatsu-sha (Kurainatama Taisha) * Inari Gosha (Inari Ōkami) * Sugawara-sha (Sugawara Taisha) * Daikoku-miya ({{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}})
== Festivals == The annual autumn festival is held on the second Sunday of October, with a preparatory night festival on the preceding Saturday. Festivities include mikoshi (portable shrines) and {{ill|Futondaiko|ja|布団太鼓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futondaiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ふとん太鼓|1=futon daiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11277203}} processions. A traditional boat procession was previously part of the celebrations but is no longer conducted. Other festivals, such as Setsubun and summer festivals, are also held.
=== Futon Daiko === The original futon daiko was lost in a storage fire, leaving only mikoshi for the autumn festival for many years. The current futon daiko was acquired from {{ill|Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|ja|岩屋神社 (明石市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|qq=|1=Iwaya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11474394}}, which donated it after commissioning a new one. The "Futon Daiko Maiko Association" oversees its management and parade.
== Access == * Nearest station: [[:en:Nishi-Maiko Station|Nishi-Maiko Station]] on the [[:en:Sanyo Electric Railway|Sanyo Electric Railway]], 3 minutes on foot; or [[:en:Maiko Station|Maiko Station]] on the JR line, 7 minutes on foot. * Parking available.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Futondaiko|ja|布団太鼓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futondaiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ふとん太鼓|1=futon daiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11277203}} * [[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]]
== External Links == * [https://rokujinjya.jp/ Official Website]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:舞子六神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11613612]] Wikidata name: Maikoroku-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| | name = Maiko Roku Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Maiko rokujinzya.JPG | location = [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[:en:Kobe|Kobe City]], [[:en:Tarumi-ku, Kobe|Tarumi Ward]], Nishi-Maiko 1-5-7 | coordinates = {{Coord|34.637222|N|135.028889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}; {{ill|Hiruko|WD=Q1067028}}; {{ill|Izanagi|WD=Q813858}}; {{ill|Izanami|WD=Q682306}}; {{ill|Susanoo|WD=Q272993}}; {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q595520}} | type = | consecration_year = | Main Hall = | alternate_name = | festival = Second Saturday and Sunday of October (weekend before Sports Day) | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Kobe#Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=38|lat_sec=14|lon_deg=135|lon_min=1|lon_sec=44|label=Maiko Roku Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} | location = {{ill|Tarumi-ku|WD=Q1078806}} }}
'''Maiko Roku Shrine''' (舞子六神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tarumi-ku, Kobe|Tarumi Ward]], [[:en:Kobe|Kobe City]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. The shrine features large stone statues of {{ill|Ebisu (god)|ja|戎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu (god)|qq=|1=Ebisu (god)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495340}} and [[:en:Daikokuten|Daikokuten]], which are said to be the largest in Japan.
== History == The shrine's foundation can be traced back to [[:en:Genroku|1689]] (Genroku 2), as evidenced by inscriptions on the golden staff carried during the mikoshi procession. However, due to the loss of historical records, details prior to this period remain unclear. The shrine's deities are the same as those of the {{ill|Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|ja|岩屋神社 (明石市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|qq=|1=Iwaya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11474394}} in Akashi, suggesting that its foundation dates to the early Edo period, when spirits from Iwaya Shrine were enshrined here. The shrine served as the general {{ill|Chinju|ja|鎮守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinju|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎮守神|1=chinjugami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97479976}} (village guardian) of Yamada Village in Harima Province.
Following the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) decree in the early Meiji era, the shrine's name changed from "Rokusha Daimyōjin" to "Rokusha Shrine" in 1874 (Meiji 7). It was later renamed "Roku Shrine," and the current main hall, rebuilt in 1890 (Meiji 23), was renovated with a copper roof between 1906 and 1907.
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞男大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tsukuyomi|ja|月夜見大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ}} * {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to えびす|1=Ebisu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1129330}}
== Sub-Shrines == * Ōtoshisha (豊宇気比賣命) * Shirahige-sha (Kifune Taisha) * Ebisu-miya (Hiruko Taisha) * Inari Omatsu-sha (Kurainatama Taisha) * Inari Gosha (Inari Ōkami) * Sugawara-sha (Sugawara Taisha) * Daikoku-miya ({{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}})
== Festivals == The annual autumn festival is held on the second Sunday of October, with a preparatory night festival on the preceding Saturday. Festivities include mikoshi (portable shrines) and {{ill|Futondaiko|ja|布団太鼓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futondaiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ふとん太鼓|1=futon daiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11277203}} processions. A traditional boat procession was previously part of the celebrations but is no longer conducted. Other festivals, such as Setsubun and summer festivals, are also held.
=== Futon Daiko === The original futon daiko was lost in a storage fire, leaving only mikoshi for the autumn festival for many years. The current futon daiko was acquired from {{ill|Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|ja|岩屋神社 (明石市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|qq=|1=Iwaya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11474394}}, which donated it after commissioning a new one. The "Futon Daiko Maiko Association" oversees its management and parade.
== Access == * Nearest station: [[:en:Nishi-Maiko Station|Nishi-Maiko Station]] on the [[:en:Sanyo Electric Railway|Sanyo Electric Railway]], 3 minutes on foot; or [[:en:Maiko Station|Maiko Station]] on the JR line, 7 minutes on foot. * Parking available.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Futondaiko|ja|布団太鼓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futondaiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ふとん太鼓|1=futon daiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11277203}} * [[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]]
== External Links == * [https://rokujinjya.jp/ Official Website]

However, due to the loss of historical records, details prior to this period remain unclear. The shrine's deities are the same as those of the {{ill|Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|ja|岩屋神社 (明石市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|1=Iwaya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11474394}} in Akashi, suggesting that its foundation dates to the early Edo period, when spirits from Iwaya Shrine were enshrined here. The shrine served as the general {{ill|Chinju|ja|鎮守|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinju|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎮守神|1=chinjugami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97479976}} (village guardian) of Yamada Village in Harima Province.
Following the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) decree in the early Meiji era, the shrine's name changed from "Rokusha Daimyōjin" to "Rokusha Shrine" in 1874 (Meiji 7). It was later renamed "Roku Shrine," and the current main hall, rebuilt in 1890 (Meiji 23), was renovated with a copper roof between 1906 and 1907.

== Sub-Shrines == * Ōtoshisha (豊宇気比賣命) * Shirahige-sha (Kifune Taisha) * Ebisu-miya (Hiruko Taisha) * Inari Omatsu-sha (Kurainatama Taisha) * Inari Gosha (Inari Ōkami) * Sugawara-sha (Sugawara Taisha) * Daikoku-miya ({{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}})
== Festivals == The annual autumn festival is held on the second Sunday of October, with a preparatory night festival on the preceding Saturday. Festivities include mikoshi (portable shrines) and {{ill|Futondaiko|ja|布団太鼓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futondaiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ふとん太鼓|1=futon daiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11277203}} processions. A traditional boat procession was previously part of the celebrations but is no longer conducted. Other festivals, such as Setsubun and summer festivals, are also held.
=== Futon Daiko === The original futon daiko was lost in a storage fire, leaving only mikoshi for the autumn festival for many years. The current futon daiko was acquired from {{ill|Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|ja|岩屋神社 (明石市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|1=Iwaya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11474394}}, which donated it after commissioning a new one. The "Futon Daiko Maiko Association" oversees its management and parade.

'''Maiko Roku Shrine''' (舞子六神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tarumi-ku, Kobe|Tarumi Ward]], [[:en:Kobe|Kobe City]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. The shrine features large stone statues of {{ill|Ebisu (god)|ja|戎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu (god)|1=Ebisu (god)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495340}} and [[:en:Daikokuten|Daikokuten]], which are said to be the largest in Japan.
== History == The shrine's foundation can be traced back to [[:en:Genroku|1689]] (Genroku 2), as evidenced by inscriptions on the golden staff carried during the mikoshi procession. However, due to the loss of historical records, details prior to this period remain unclear. The shrine's deities are the same as those of the {{ill|Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|ja|岩屋神社 (明石市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|1=Iwaya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11474394}} in Akashi, suggesting that its foundation dates to the early Edo period, when spirits from Iwaya Shrine were enshrined here. The shrine served as the general {{ill|Chinju|ja|鎮守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinju|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎮守神|1=chinjugami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97479976}} (village guardian) of Yamada Village in Harima Province.
Following the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) decree in the early Meiji era, the shrine's name changed from "Rokusha Daimyōjin" to "Rokusha Shrine" in 1874 (Meiji 7). It was later renamed "Roku Shrine," and the current main hall, rebuilt in 1890 (Meiji 23), was renovated with a copper roof between 1906 and 1907.

== Sub-Shrines == * Ōtoshisha (豊宇気比賣命) * Shirahige-sha (Kifune Taisha) * Ebisu-miya (Hiruko Taisha) * Inari Omatsu-sha (Kurainatama Taisha) * Inari Gosha (Inari Ōkami) * Sugawara-sha (Sugawara Taisha) * Daikoku-miya ({{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}})
== Festivals == The annual autumn festival is held on the second Sunday of October, with a preparatory night festival on the preceding Saturday. Festivities include mikoshi (portable shrines) and {{ill|Futondaiko|ja|布団太鼓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futondaiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ふとん太鼓|1=futon daiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11277203}} processions. A traditional boat procession was previously part of the celebrations but is no longer conducted. Other festivals, such as Setsubun and summer festivals, are also held.
=== Futon Daiko === The original futon daiko was lost in a storage fire, leaving only mikoshi for the autumn festival for many years. The current futon daiko was acquired from {{ill|Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|ja|岩屋神社 (明石市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|1=Iwaya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11474394}}, which donated it after commissioning a new one. The "Futon Daiko Maiko Association" oversees its management and parade.

== Related Articles == * {{ill|Futondaiko|ja|布団太鼓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futondaiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ふとん太鼓|1=futon daiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11277203}} * [[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:舞子六神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11613612]] Wikidata name: Maikoroku-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| | name = Maiko Roku Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Maiko rokujinzya.JPG | location = [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[:en:Kobe|Kobe City]], [[:en:Tarumi-ku, Kobe|Tarumi Ward]], Nishi-Maiko 1-5-7 | coordinates = {{Coord|34.637222|N|135.028889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}; {{ill|Hiruko|WD=Q1067028}}; {{ill|Izanagi|WD=Q813858}}; {{ill|Izanami|WD=Q682306}}; {{ill|Susanoo|WD=Q272993}}; {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q595520}} | type = | consecration_year = | Main Hall = | alternate_name = | festival = Second Saturday and Sunday of October (weekend before Sports Day) | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Kobe#Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=38|lat_sec=14|lon_deg=135|lon_min=1|lon_sec=44|label=Maiko Roku Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} | location = {{ill|Tarumi-ku|WD=Q1078806}} }}
'''Maiko Roku Shrine''' (舞子六神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tarumi-ku, Kobe|Tarumi Ward]], [[:en:Kobe|Kobe City]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. The shrine features large stone statues of {{ill|Ebisu (god)|ja|戎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu (god)|1=Ebisu (god)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495340}} and [[:en:Daikokuten|Daikokuten]], which are said to be the largest in Japan.
== History == The shrine's foundation can be traced back to [[:en:Genroku|1689]] (Genroku 2), as evidenced by inscriptions on the golden staff carried during the mikoshi procession. However, due to the loss of historical records, details prior to this period remain unclear. The shrine's deities are the same as those of the {{ill|Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|ja|岩屋神社 (明石市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|1=Iwaya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11474394}} in Akashi, suggesting that its foundation dates to the early Edo period, when spirits from Iwaya Shrine were enshrined here. The shrine served as the general {{ill|Chinju|ja|鎮守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinju|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎮守神|1=chinjugami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97479976}} (village guardian) of Yamada Village in Harima Province.
Following the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) decree in the early Meiji era, the shrine's name changed from "Rokusha Daimyōjin" to "Rokusha Shrine" in 1874 (Meiji 7). It was later renamed "Roku Shrine," and the current main hall, rebuilt in 1890 (Meiji 23), was renovated with a copper roof between 1906 and 1907.
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞男大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tsukuyomi|ja|月夜見大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ}} * {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to えびす|1=Ebisu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1129330}}
== Sub-Shrines == * Ōtoshisha (豊宇気比賣命) * Shirahige-sha (Kifune Taisha) * Ebisu-miya (Hiruko Taisha) * Inari Omatsu-sha (Kurainatama Taisha) * Inari Gosha (Inari Ōkami) * Sugawara-sha (Sugawara Taisha) * Daikoku-miya ({{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}})
== Festivals == The annual autumn festival is held on the second Sunday of October, with a preparatory night festival on the preceding Saturday. Festivities include mikoshi (portable shrines) and {{ill|Futondaiko|ja|布団太鼓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futondaiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ふとん太鼓|1=futon daiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11277203}} processions. A traditional boat procession was previously part of the celebrations but is no longer conducted. Other festivals, such as Setsubun and summer festivals, are also held.
=== Futon Daiko === The original futon daiko was lost in a storage fire, leaving only mikoshi for the autumn festival for many years. The current futon daiko was acquired from {{ill|Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|ja|岩屋神社 (明石市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|1=Iwaya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11474394}}, which donated it after commissioning a new one. The "Futon Daiko Maiko Association" oversees its management and parade.
== Access == * Nearest station: [[:en:Nishi-Maiko Station|Nishi-Maiko Station]] on the [[:en:Sanyo Electric Railway|Sanyo Electric Railway]], 3 minutes on foot; or [[:en:Maiko Station|Maiko Station]] on the JR line, 7 minutes on foot. * Parking available.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Futondaiko|ja|布団太鼓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futondaiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ふとん太鼓|1=futon daiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11277203}} * [[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]]
== External Links == * [https://rokujinjya.jp/ Official Website]

'''Maiko Roku Shrine''' (舞子六神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tarumi-ku, Kobe|Tarumi Ward]], [[:en:Kobe|Kobe City]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. The shrine features large stone statues of {{ill|Ebisu (god)|ja|戎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu (god)|1=Ebisu (god)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495340}} and [[:en:Daikokuten|Daikokuten]], which are said to be the largest in Japan.
== History == The shrine's foundation can be traced back to [[:en:Genroku|1689]] (Genroku 2), as evidenced by inscriptions on the golden staff carried during the mikoshi procession. However, due to the loss of historical records, details prior to this period remain unclear. The shrine's deities are the same as those of the {{ill|Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|ja|岩屋神社 (明石市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|1=Iwaya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11474394}} in Akashi, suggesting that its foundation dates to the early Edo period, when spirits from Iwaya Shrine were enshrined here. The shrine served as the general {{ill|Chinju|ja|鎮守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinju|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎮守神|1=chinjugami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97479976}} (village guardian) of Yamada Village in Harima Province.
Following the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) decree in the early Meiji era, the shrine's name changed from "Rokusha Daimyōjin" to "Rokusha Shrine" in 1874 (Meiji 7). It was later renamed "Roku Shrine," and the current main hall, rebuilt in 1890 (Meiji 23), was renovated with a copper roof between 1906 and 1907.
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞男大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tsukuyomi|ja|月夜見大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ}} * {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to えびす|1=Ebisu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1129330}}
== Sub-Shrines == * Ōtoshisha (豊宇気比賣命) * Shirahige-sha (Kifune Taisha) * Ebisu-miya (Hiruko Taisha) * Inari Omatsu-sha (Kurainatama Taisha) * Inari Gosha (Inari Ōkami) * Sugawara-sha (Sugawara Taisha) * Daikoku-miya ({{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}})
== Festivals == The annual autumn festival is held on the second Sunday of October, with a preparatory night festival on the preceding Saturday. Festivities include mikoshi (portable shrines) and {{ill|Futondaiko|ja|布団太鼓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futondaiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ふとん太鼓|1=futon daiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11277203}} processions. A traditional boat procession was previously part of the celebrations but is no longer conducted. Other festivals, such as Setsubun and summer festivals, are also held.
=== Futon Daiko === The original futon daiko was lost in a storage fire, leaving only mikoshi for the autumn festival for many years. The current futon daiko was acquired from {{ill|Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|ja|岩屋神社 (明石市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|1=Iwaya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11474394}}, which donated it after commissioning a new one. The "Futon Daiko Maiko Association" oversees its management and parade.
== Access == * Nearest station: [[:en:Nishi-Maiko Station|Nishi-Maiko Station]] on the [[:en:Sanyo Electric Railway|Sanyo Electric Railway]], 3 minutes on foot; or [[:en:Maiko Station|Maiko Station]] on the JR line, 7 minutes on foot. * Parking available.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Futondaiko|ja|布団太鼓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futondaiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ふとん太鼓|1=futon daiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11277203}} * [[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]]
== External Links == * [https://rokujinjya.jp/ Official Website]

However, due to the loss of historical records, details prior to this period remain unclear. The shrine served as the general {{ill|Chinju|ja|鎮守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinju|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎮守神|1=chinjugami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97479976}} (village guardian) of Yamada Village in Harima Province.
Following the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) decree in the early Meiji era, the shrine's name changed from "Rokusha Daimyōjin" to "Rokusha Shrine" in 1874 (Meiji 7). It was later renamed "Roku Shrine," and the current main hall, rebuilt in 1890 (Meiji 23), was renovated with a copper roof between 1906 and 1907.
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞男大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tsukuyomi|ja|月夜見大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ}} * {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to えびす|1=Ebisu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1129330}}
== Sub-Shrines == * Ōtoshisha (豊宇気比賣命) * Shirahige-sha (Kifune Taisha) * Ebisu-miya (Hiruko Taisha) * Inari Omatsu-sha (Kurainatama Taisha) * Inari Gosha (Inari Ōkami) * Sugawara-sha (Sugawara Taisha) * Daikoku-miya ({{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}})
== Festivals == The annual autumn festival is held on the second Sunday of October, with a preparatory night festival on the preceding Saturday. Festivities include mikoshi (portable shrines) and {{ill|Futondaiko|ja|布団太鼓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futondaiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ふとん太鼓|1=futon daiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11277203}} processions. A traditional boat procession was previously part of the celebrations but is no longer conducted. Other festivals, such as Setsubun and summer festivals, are also held.
=== Futon Daiko === The original futon daiko was lost in a storage fire, leaving only mikoshi for the autumn festival for many years. The current futon daiko was acquired from {{ill|Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|ja|岩屋神社 (明石市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|1=Iwaya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11474394}}, which donated it after commissioning a new one. The "Futon Daiko Maiko Association" oversees its management and parade.
== Access == * Nearest station: [[:en:Nishi-Maiko Station|Nishi-Maiko Station]] on the [[:en:Sanyo Electric Railway|Sanyo Electric Railway]], 3 minutes on foot; or [[:en:Maiko Station|Maiko Station]] on the JR line, 7 minutes on foot. * Parking available.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Futondaiko|ja|布団太鼓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futondaiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ふとん太鼓|1=futon daiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11277203}} * [[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]]

'''Maiko Roku Shrine''' (舞子六神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Tarumi-ku, Kobe|en|Tarumi-ku, Kobe|WD=Q1078806|lt=Tarumi Ward}}, {{ill|Kobe|en|Kobe|WD=Q48320|lt=Kobe City}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. The shrine features large stone statues of {{ill|Ebisu (god)|ja|戎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu (god)|1=Ebisu (god)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495340}} and {{ill|Daikokuten|en|Daikokuten|WD=Q1759916|lt=Daikokuten}}, which are said to be the largest in Japan.
== History == The shrine's foundation can be traced back to {{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|WD=Q1325001|lt=1689}} (Genroku 2), as evidenced by inscriptions on the golden staff carried during the mikoshi procession. However, due to the loss of historical records, details prior to this period remain unclear. The shrine's deities are the same as those of the {{ill|Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|ja|岩屋神社 (明石市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|1=Iwaya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11474394}} in Akashi, suggesting that its foundation dates to the early Edo period, when spirits from Iwaya Shrine were enshrined here. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) decree in the early Meiji era, the shrine's name changed from "Rokusha Daimyōjin" to "Rokusha Shrine" in 1874 (Meiji 7). It was later renamed "Roku Shrine," and the current main hall, rebuilt in 1890 (Meiji 23), was renovated with a copper roof between 1906 and 1907.

== Festivals == The annual autumn festival is held on the second Sunday of October, with a preparatory night festival on the preceding Saturday. Festivities include mikoshi (portable shrines) and {{ill|Futondaiko|ja|布団太鼓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futondaiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ふとん太鼓|1=futon daiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11277203}} processions. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Futondaiko|ja|布団太鼓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futondaiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ふとん太鼓|1=futon daiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11277203}} * {{ill|Mikoshi|en|Mikoshi|WD=Q979940|lt=Mikoshi}}

== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞男大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tsukuyomi|ja|月夜見大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ}} * {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to えびす|1=Ebisu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1129330}}
== Sub-Shrines == * Ōtoshisha (豊宇気比賣命) * Shirahige-sha (Kifune Taisha) * Ebisu-miya (Hiruko Taisha) * Inari Omatsu-sha (Kurainatama Taisha) * Inari Gosha (Inari Ōkami) * Sugawara-sha (Sugawara Taisha) * Daikoku-miya ({{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}})
== Festivals == The annual autumn festival is held on the second Sunday of October, with a preparatory night festival on the preceding Saturday. Festivities include mikoshi (portable shrines) and {{ill|Futondaiko|ja|布団太鼓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futondaiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ふとん太鼓|1=futon daiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11277203}} processions. A traditional boat procession was previously part of the celebrations but is no longer conducted. Other festivals, such as Setsubun and summer festivals, are also held.
=== Futon Daiko === The original futon daiko was lost in a storage fire, leaving only mikoshi for the autumn festival for many years. The current futon daiko was acquired from {{ill|Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|ja|岩屋神社 (明石市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|1=Iwaya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11474394}}, which donated it after commissioning a new one. The "Futon Daiko Maiko Association" oversees its management and parade.
== Access == * Nearest station: {{ill|Nishi-Maiko Station|en|Nishi-Maiko Station|WD=Q11629438|lt=Nishi-Maiko Station}} on the {{ill|Sanyo Electric Railway|en|Sanyo Electric Railway|WD=Q2245513|lt=Sanyo Electric Railway}}, 3 minutes on foot; or {{ill|Maiko Station|en|Maiko Station|WD=Q1042776|lt=Maiko Station}} on the JR line, 7 minutes on foot. * Parking available.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Futondaiko|ja|布団太鼓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futondaiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ふとん太鼓|1=futon daiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11277203}} * {{ill|Mikoshi|en|Mikoshi|WD=Q979940|lt=Mikoshi}}
== External Links == * [https://rokujinjya.jp/ Official Website]

{{Infobox religious building| | name = Maiko Roku Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Maiko rokujinzya.JPG | location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kobe|en|Kobe|WD=Q48320|lt=Kobe City}}, {{ill|Tarumi-ku, Kobe|en|Tarumi-ku, Kobe|WD=Q1078806|lt=Tarumi Ward}}, Nishi-Maiko 1-5-7 | coordinates = {{Coord|34.637222|N|135.028889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}; {{ill|Hiruko|WD=Q1067028}}; {{ill|Izanagi|WD=Q813858}}; {{ill|Izanami|WD=Q682306}}; {{ill|Susanoo|WD=Q272993}}; {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q595520}} | type = | consecration_year = | Main Hall = | alternate_name = | festival = Second Saturday and Sunday of October (weekend before Sports Day) | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Kobe#Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=38|lat_sec=14|lon_deg=135|lon_min=1|lon_sec=44|label=Maiko Roku Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} | location = {{ill|Tarumi-ku|WD=Q1078806}} }}
'''Maiko Roku Shrine''' (舞子六神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Tarumi-ku, Kobe|en|Tarumi-ku, Kobe|WD=Q1078806|lt=Tarumi Ward}}, {{ill|Kobe|en|Kobe|WD=Q48320|lt=Kobe City}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. The shrine features large stone statues of {{ill|Ebisu (god)|ja|戎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu (god)|1=Ebisu (god)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495340}} and {{ill|Daikokuten|en|Daikokuten|WD=Q1759916|lt=Daikokuten}}, which are said to be the largest in Japan.
== History == The shrine's foundation can be traced back to {{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|WD=Q1325001|lt=1689}} (Genroku 2), as evidenced by inscriptions on the golden staff carried during the mikoshi procession. However, due to the loss of historical records, details prior to this period remain unclear. The shrine's deities are the same as those of the {{ill|Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|ja|岩屋神社 (明石市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|1=Iwaya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11474394}} in Akashi, suggesting that its foundation dates to the early Edo period, when spirits from Iwaya Shrine were enshrined here. The shrine served as the general {{ill|Chinju|ja|鎮守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinju|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎮守神|1=chinjugami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97479976}} (village guardian) of Yamada Village in Harima Province.
Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) decree in the early Meiji era, the shrine's name changed from "Rokusha Daimyōjin" to "Rokusha Shrine" in 1874 (Meiji 7). It was later renamed "Roku Shrine," and the current main hall, rebuilt in 1890 (Meiji 23), was renovated with a copper roof between 1906 and 1907.
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞男大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tsukuyomi|ja|月夜見大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ}} * {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to えびす|1=Ebisu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1129330}}
== Sub-Shrines == * Ōtoshisha (豊宇気比賣命) * Shirahige-sha (Kifune Taisha) * Ebisu-miya (Hiruko Taisha) * Inari Omatsu-sha (Kurainatama Taisha) * Inari Gosha (Inari Ōkami) * Sugawara-sha (Sugawara Taisha) * Daikoku-miya ({{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}})
== Festivals == The annual autumn festival is held on the second Sunday of October, with a preparatory night festival on the preceding Saturday. Festivities include mikoshi (portable shrines) and {{ill|Futondaiko|ja|布団太鼓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futondaiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ふとん太鼓|1=futon daiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11277203}} processions. A traditional boat procession was previously part of the celebrations but is no longer conducted. Other festivals, such as Setsubun and summer festivals, are also held.
=== Futon Daiko === The original futon daiko was lost in a storage fire, leaving only mikoshi for the autumn festival for many years. The current futon daiko was acquired from {{ill|Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|ja|岩屋神社 (明石市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|1=Iwaya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11474394}}, which donated it after commissioning a new one. The "Futon Daiko Maiko Association" oversees its management and parade.
== Access == * Nearest station: {{ill|Nishi-Maiko Station|en|Nishi-Maiko Station|WD=Q11629438|lt=Nishi-Maiko Station}} on the {{ill|Sanyo Electric Railway|en|Sanyo Electric Railway|WD=Q2245513|lt=Sanyo Electric Railway}}, 3 minutes on foot; or {{ill|Maiko Station|en|Maiko Station|WD=Q1042776|lt=Maiko Station}} on the JR line, 7 minutes on foot. * Parking available.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Futondaiko|ja|布団太鼓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futondaiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ふとん太鼓|1=futon daiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11277203}} * {{ill|Mikoshi|en|Mikoshi|WD=Q979940|lt=Mikoshi}}

== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞男大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi|ja|月夜見大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ}} * {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to えびす|1=Ebisu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1129330}}
== Sub-Shrines == * Ōtoshisha (豊宇気比賣命) * Shirahige-sha (Kifune Taisha) * Ebisu-miya (Hiruko Taisha) * Inari Omatsu-sha (Kurainatama Taisha) * Inari Gosha (Inari Ōkami) * Sugawara-sha (Sugawara Taisha) * Daikoku-miya ({{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}})
== Festivals == The annual autumn festival is held on the second Sunday of October, with a preparatory night festival on the preceding Saturday. Festivities include mikoshi (portable shrines) and {{ill|Futondaiko|ja|布団太鼓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futondaiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ふとん太鼓|1=futon daiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11277203}} processions. A traditional boat procession was previously part of the celebrations but is no longer conducted. Other festivals, such as Setsubun and summer festivals, are also held.
=== Futon Daiko === The original futon daiko was lost in a storage fire, leaving only mikoshi for the autumn festival for many years. The current futon daiko was acquired from {{ill|Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|ja|岩屋神社 (明石市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|1=Iwaya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11474394}}, which donated it after commissioning a new one. The "Futon Daiko Maiko Association" oversees its management and parade.
== Access == * Nearest station: {{ill|Nishi-Maiko Station|en|Nishi-Maiko Station|WD=Q11629438|lt=Nishi-Maiko Station}} on the {{ill|Sanyo Electric Railway|en|Sanyo Electric Railway|WD=Q2245513|lt=Sanyo Electric Railway}}, 3 minutes on foot; or {{ill|Maiko Station|en|Maiko Station|WD=Q1042776|lt=Maiko Station}} on the JR line, 7 minutes on foot. * Parking available.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Futondaiko|ja|布団太鼓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futondaiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ふとん太鼓|1=futon daiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11277203}} * {{ill|Mikoshi|en|Mikoshi|WD=Q979940|lt=Mikoshi}}

{{Infobox religious building| | name = Maiko Roku Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Maiko rokujinzya.JPG | location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kobe|en|Kobe|WD=Q48320|lt=Kobe City|1=Kobe}}, {{ill|Tarumi-ku, Kobe|en|Tarumi-ku, Kobe|WD=Q1078806|lt=Tarumi Ward|1=Tarumi-ku, Kobe}}, Nishi-Maiko 1-5-7 | coordinates = {{Coord|34.637222|N|135.028889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}; {{ill|Hiruko|WD=Q1067028|1=Hiruko}}; {{ill|Izanagi|WD=Q813858|1=Izanagi}}; {{ill|Izanami|WD=Q682306|1=Izanami}}; {{ill|Susanoo|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}; {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q595520|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}} | type = | consecration_year = | Main Hall = | alternate_name = | festival = Second Saturday and Sunday of October (weekend before Sports Day) | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Kobe#Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=38|lat_sec=14|lon_deg=135|lon_min=1|lon_sec=44|label=Maiko Roku Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} | location = {{ill|Tarumi-ku|WD=Q1078806|1=Tarumi-ku, Kobe}} }}
'''Maiko Roku Shrine''' (舞子六神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Tarumi-ku, Kobe|en|Tarumi-ku, Kobe|WD=Q1078806|lt=Tarumi Ward|1=Tarumi-ku, Kobe}}, {{ill|Kobe|en|Kobe|WD=Q48320|lt=Kobe City|1=Kobe}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. The shrine features large stone statues of {{ill|Ebisu (god)|ja|戎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu (god)|1=Q11495340|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495340}} and {{ill|Daikokuten|en|Daikokuten|WD=Q1759916|lt=Daikokuten|1=Daikokuten}}, which are said to be the largest in Japan.
== History == The shrine's foundation can be traced back to {{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|WD=Q1325001|lt=1689|1=Genroku}} (Genroku 2), as evidenced by inscriptions on the golden staff carried during the mikoshi procession. However, due to the loss of historical records, details prior to this period remain unclear. The shrine's deities are the same as those of the {{ill|Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|ja|岩屋神社 (明石市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|1=Iwaya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11474394}} in Akashi, suggesting that its foundation dates to the early Edo period, when spirits from Iwaya Shrine were enshrined here. The shrine served as the general {{ill|Chinju|ja|鎮守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinju|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎮守神|1=Chinjugami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97479976}} (village guardian) of Yamada Village in Harima Province.
Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri|1=Shinbutsu bunri}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) decree in the early Meiji era, the shrine's name changed from "Rokusha Daimyōjin" to "Rokusha Shrine" in 1874 (Meiji 7). It was later renamed "Roku Shrine," and the current main hall, rebuilt in 1890 (Meiji 23), was renovated with a copper roof between 1906 and 1907.
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞男大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi|ja|月夜見大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to えびす|1=Ebisu (mythology)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1129330}}
== Sub-Shrines == * Ōtoshisha (豊宇気比賣命) * Shirahige-sha (Kifune Taisha) * Ebisu-miya (Hiruko Taisha) * Inari Omatsu-sha (Kurainatama Taisha) * Inari Gosha (Inari Ōkami) * Sugawara-sha (Sugawara Taisha) * Daikoku-miya ({{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}})
== Festivals == The annual autumn festival is held on the second Sunday of October, with a preparatory night festival on the preceding Saturday. Festivities include mikoshi (portable shrines) and {{ill|Futondaiko|ja|布団太鼓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futondaiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ふとん太鼓|1=Futon Daiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11277203}} processions. A traditional boat procession was previously part of the celebrations but is no longer conducted. Other festivals, such as Setsubun and summer festivals, are also held.
=== Futon Daiko === The original futon daiko was lost in a storage fire, leaving only mikoshi for the autumn festival for many years. The current futon daiko was acquired from {{ill|Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|ja|岩屋神社 (明石市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|1=Iwaya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11474394}}, which donated it after commissioning a new one. The "Futon Daiko Maiko Association" oversees its management and parade.
== Access == * Nearest station: {{ill|Nishi-Maiko Station|en|Nishi-Maiko Station|WD=Q11629438|lt=Nishi-Maiko Station|1=Nishi-Maiko Station}} on the {{ill|Sanyo Electric Railway|en|Sanyo Electric Railway|WD=Q2245513|lt=Sanyo Electric Railway|1=Sanyo Electric Railway}}, 3 minutes on foot; or {{ill|Maiko Station|en|Maiko Station|WD=Q1042776|lt=Maiko Station|1=Maiko Station}} on the JR line, 7 minutes on foot. * Parking available.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Futondaiko|ja|布団太鼓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futondaiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ふとん太鼓|1=Futon Daiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11277203}} * {{ill|Mikoshi|en|Mikoshi|WD=Q979940|lt=Mikoshi|1=Mikoshi}}

The shrine's deities are the same as those of the {{ill|Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|ja|岩屋神社 (明石市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|1=Iwaya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11474394}} in Akashi, suggesting that its foundation dates to the early Edo period, when spirits from Iwaya Shrine were enshrined here. The shrine served as the general {{ill|Chinju|ja|鎮守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinju|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎮守神|1=Chinjugami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97479976}} (village guardian) of Yamada Village in Harima Province.
Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri|1=Shinbutsu bunri}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) decree in the early Meiji era, the shrine's name changed from "Rokusha Daimyōjin" to "Rokusha Shrine" in 1874 (Meiji 7). It was later renamed "Roku Shrine," and the current main hall, rebuilt in 1890 (Meiji 23), was renovated with a copper roof between 1906 and 1907.
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞男大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi|ja|月夜見大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to えびす|1=Ebisu (mythology)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1129330}}
== Sub-Shrines == * Ōtoshisha (豊宇気比賣命) * Shirahige-sha (Kifune Taisha) * Ebisu-miya (Hiruko Taisha) * Inari Omatsu-sha (Kurainatama Taisha) * Inari Gosha (Inari Ōkami) * Sugawara-sha (Sugawara Taisha) * Daikoku-miya ({{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}})
== Festivals == The annual autumn festival is held on the second Sunday of October, with a preparatory night festival on the preceding Saturday. Festivities include mikoshi (portable shrines) and {{ill|Futondaiko|ja|布団太鼓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futondaiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ふとん太鼓|1=Futon Daiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11277203}} processions. A traditional boat procession was previously part of the celebrations but is no longer conducted. Other festivals, such as Setsubun and summer festivals, are also held.
=== Futon Daiko === The original futon daiko was lost in a storage fire, leaving only mikoshi for the autumn festival for many years. The current futon daiko was acquired from {{ill|Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|ja|岩屋神社 (明石市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|1=Iwaya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11474394}}, which donated it after commissioning a new one. The "Futon Daiko Maiko Association" oversees its management and parade.
== Access == * Nearest station: {{ill|Nishi-Maiko Station|en|Nishi-Maiko Station|WD=Q11629438|lt=Nishi-Maiko Station|1=Nishi-Maiko Station}} on the {{ill|Sanyo Electric Railway|en|Sanyo Electric Railway|WD=Q2245513|lt=Sanyo Electric Railway|1=Sanyo Electric Railway}}, 3 minutes on foot; or {{ill|Maiko Station|en|Maiko Station|WD=Q1042776|lt=Maiko Station|1=Maiko Station}} on the JR line, 7 minutes on foot. * Parking available.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Futondaiko|ja|布団太鼓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futondaiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ふとん太鼓|1=Futon Daiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11277203}} * {{ill|Mikoshi|en|Mikoshi|WD=Q979940|lt=Mikoshi|1=Mikoshi}}
== External Links == * [https://rokujinjya.jp/ Official Website]

'''Maiko Roku Shrine''' (舞子六神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Tarumi-ku, Kobe|en|Tarumi-ku, Kobe|WD=Q1078806|lt=Tarumi Ward|1=Tarumi-ku, Kobe}}, {{ill|Kobe|en|Kobe|WD=Q48320|lt=Kobe City|1=Kobe}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. The shrine features large stone statues of {{ill|Ebisu (god)|ja|戎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu (god)|1=Q11495340|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495340}} and {{ill|Daikokuten|en|Daikokuten|WD=Q1759916|lt=Daikokuten|1=Daikokuten}}, which are said to be the largest in Japan.
== History == The shrine's foundation can be traced back to {{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|WD=Q1325001|lt=1689|1=Genroku}} (Genroku 2), as evidenced by inscriptions on the golden staff carried during the mikoshi procession. However, due to the loss of historical records, details prior to this period remain unclear. The shrine's deities are the same as those of the {{ill|Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|ja|岩屋神社 (明石市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|1=Iwaya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11474394}} in Akashi, suggesting that its foundation dates to the early Edo period, when spirits from Iwaya Shrine were enshrined here. The shrine served as the general {{ill|Chinju|ja|鎮守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinju|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎮守神|1=Chinjugami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97479976}} (village guardian) of Yamada Village in Harima Province.
Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri|1=Shinbutsu bunri}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) decree in the early Meiji era, the shrine's name changed from "Rokusha Daimyōjin" to "Rokusha Shrine" in 1874 (Meiji 7). It was later renamed "Roku Shrine," and the current main hall, rebuilt in 1890 (Meiji 23), was renovated with a copper roof between 1906 and 1907.
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞男大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi|ja|月夜見大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to えびす|1=Ebisu (mythology)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1129330}}

A traditional boat procession was previously part of the celebrations but is no longer conducted. Other festivals, such as Setsubun and summer festivals, are also held.

The current futon daiko was acquired from {{ill|Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|ja|岩屋神社 (明石市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaya Shrine (Akashi)|1=Iwaya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11474394}}, which donated it after commissioning a new one. The "Futon Daiko Maiko Association" oversees its management and parade.
